message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

[phaseprompt] Read ui-audit/02-comparison.md including any reconciliation log appended to it. Also read ui-audit/03-verification.md for context. Do not re-read source code at this stage. Do not read prior audit pass files; the comparison file is now the consolidated source.

My working hypothesis for the target architecture, which you should treat as a hypothesis to test against the evidence rather than as a conclusion: the application shell meaning top bar left navigation routing and theme should be shared across all modules; each module should share an underlying framework implemented uniformly; the main canvas or work area should share a rendering engine with per-module functionality layered on top.

Evaluate this hypothesis against the findings. Where the evidence supports it, say so and cite the relevant comparison rows. Where the evidence suggests a different or better approach, say so and explain why with reference to the comparison rows.

Produce ui-audit/04-recommendations.md with four sections. Section one, target architecture, refined from my hypothesis based on the evidence, stated as what should be true when this work is complete. Section two, per-element convergence recommendations, going through all eleven categories and stating what to standardize on, which existing implementation to adopt if any, or whether to build new, with reasoning per category. Section three, rough migration order with dependency notes, meaning which changes unblock which others. Section four, risks and unknowns, including any places the audit lacked detail or where the verification step could not confirm a claim.

You may search the web for UI consistency or design system best practices if a specific recommendation would benefit from external reference. Cite anything you use with the URL.

Do not start implementation. Do not write any code. This is a planning artifact. When done print the file path and stop.

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- audit: Verify a spec unit's implementation against its authoritative spec. Reads the manifest, reads spec files, walks target code, catalogs every gap, and writes task files for each finding.
- spec: Read the implementation spec for a given I/O module. Usage: /spec <module>. Modules: console, process, designer, graphics, display-elements, shapes, opc
- design-qa: Inventory all implementations of a cross-cutting feature across the codebase, surface inconsistencies, ask targeted design questions, and write the answers as an authoritative decision file. Run this BEFORE /audit on any feature marked ⚠️ NOT SPECCED in the manifest.
- spec-index: Print a quick-reference index of every audit unit — unit ID, canonical name, wave, and current task status. Use this to look up the correct unit ID or module name before running spec-scout or feature-agent.
- agent-guide: Print a reference guide for all custom agents in this project — what each one does, when to use it, how to launch it, and usage examples.
- wrapup: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
- review: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
- deepreview: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
- summarize: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Audit UI architecture and create recommendations

content.content:
1	# UI Audit Verification — Code-Level Fact-Check
2	
3	**Source verified against:** live frontend source code only. No design docs or audit files consulted.
4	**Date:** 2026-05-27
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Verification Method
9	
10	Every claim in the 11 category tables (5 rows each × 11 categories = 55 table rows) and 3 lists (12 + 12 + 7 = 31 items) was checked by reading the cited source files. Source of truth is the code. Comparison file (`02-comparison.md`) is not amended.
11	
12	---
13	
14	## Table of Results
15	
16	### Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens
17	
18	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
19	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
20	| All | Cat 1 | Implementation | "all colors via CSS custom properties from `index.css`; no module-specific token overrides" | YES | — |
21	| All | Cat 1 | Source-of-truth files | "`index.css:19–295` (138 tokens)" | YES | The comment in index.css itself states 138 tokens; the range 19–295 reaches through the light-theme pen colors, which is correct for Console. Designer/Settings ranges (19–219 and 17–219) correspond to the dark-theme block end and the block opening comment; minor variation but not wrong. |
22	| All | Cat 1 | Visual properties | "~20 token references; hardcoded hex for alarm priority/quality badge colors, TrendPane series palette, published dot" | YES | Confirmed: `#10b981` published dot in console/index.tsx:2034; PRIORITY_COLOR hardcoded in AlarmListPane.tsx:34-35; QualityBadge hardcoded in PointTablePane.tsx:30-34. |
23	| Designer | Cat 1 | Notes | "SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS are **fully hardcoded** rgba objects with no registry equivalent" | **NO** | `SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"` (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48) and `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"` (line 55). Two of the six entries use registered tokens. Not "fully hardcoded." |
24	| All | Cat 1 | Deviations | "`var(--io-bg)` and `var(--io-text)` referenced but not defined in `index.css`" | YES | Confirmed: neither `--io-bg` nor bare `--io-text` appears in index.css. Both are genuinely undefined. |
25	
26	### Category 2 — Typography
27	
28	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
29	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
30	| All | Cat 2 | Implementation | "All three use inline-styles exclusively; no shared typography component exists across modules" | YES | — |
31	| All | Cat 2 | Visual properties | "Raw px integers 9–18px; `fontFamily: monospace`" | YES | Groups.tsx:197 `fontFamily: "monospace"` confirmed. |
32	| All | Cat 2 | Deviations | "Zero typography scale tokens used" | YES | No file in any of the three modules references `--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`. |
33	
34	### Category 3 — Toolbars
35	
36	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
37	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
38	| Console | Cat 3 | Visual properties | "48px height; `background: var(--io-surface)`" | YES | console/index.tsx:1949-1951 confirmed. |
39	| Designer | Cat 3 | Visual properties | "DesignerToolbar 44px; mode/tab strips with 2px accent underline" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1162 `height: 44`; DesignerModeTabs.tsx:210 `height: 36`; DesignerTabBar.tsx:370 `height: 36`; DesignerStatusBar.tsx:178 `height: 28`. |
40	| Designer | Cat 3 | Notes | "Loading skeleton toolbar placeholder uses 40px height vs the actual 44px" | YES | designer/index.tsx:773 `height: 40` for skeleton toolbar row vs actual 44px. |
41	| Console | Cat 3 | Deviations | "Publish button rendered twice in right controls block (duplicate render)" | YES | console/index.tsx:2294-2325 and 2721-2752 both render the publish toggle button under `{canPublish && (...)`. |
42	
43	### Category 4 — Menus
44	
45	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
46	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
47	| All | Cat 4 | Visual properties | "ContextMenu: `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger items rather than `var(--io-danger)`" | YES | shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx:247 `? "var(--io-alarm-urgent)"` confirmed. |
48	| Designer | Cat 4 | Notes | "File menu has no Escape key handler" | YES | No `Escape`/`keydown`/`onKeyDown` handler found in DesignerModeTabs.tsx File menu code. |
49	| Designer | Cat 4 | Deviations | "zoom dropdown hover uses `var(--io-surface-hover)` (undefined token)" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1459 `e.currentTarget.style.background = "var(--io-surface-hover)"` confirmed. `--io-surface-hover` not in index.css. |
50	| Designer | Cat 4 | Deviations | "zoom font uses `var(--io-font-sans)` (undefined)" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1456 `fontFamily: "var(--io-font-sans)"` confirmed. Not in index.css. |
51	
52	### Category 5 — Side Panels
53	
54	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
55	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
56	| Console | Cat 5 | Visual properties | "220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`" | YES | ConsolePalette.tsx uses `width: "220px"` style. |
57	| Settings | Cat 5 | Visual properties | "220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`" | YES | settings/index.tsx:182 `width: "220px"` confirmed. |
58	| All | Cat 5 | Deviations | "220px vs `--io-sidebar-width: 240px`" | YES | `--io-sidebar-width: 240px` at index.css:106; all three modules use hardcoded 220px. |
59	| Settings | Cat 5 | Deviations | "active left-border accent missing" | YES | No `borderLeft`, `--io-sidebar-active-border`, or `2px solid var(--io-accent)` found in settings/index.tsx nav items. |
60	
61	### Category 6 — Buttons
62	
63	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
64	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
65	| All | Cat 6 | Shared | "none use the 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens" | YES | `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, etc. appear nowhere in console, designer, or settings page files. |
66	| Settings | Cat 6 | Visual properties | "`btnPrimary`: `var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-text-on-accent)` (undefined)" | YES | settingsStyles.ts:22-31 confirmed. `--io-text-on-accent` not in index.css. |
67	| Settings | Cat 6 | Notes | "`BulkUpdate.tsx` defines its own full button set (`BTN_PRIMARY`, `BTN_SECONDARY`, `BTN_DANGER`) independently from `settingsStyles`" | YES | BulkUpdate.tsx:33-63 confirmed. Uses `borderRadius: "6px"` string instead of `var(--io-radius)`. |
68	
69	### Category 7 — Form Inputs
70	
71	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
72	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
73	| All | Cat 7 | Shared | "All suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) without a CSS replacement" | YES | ConsolePalette, PaneConfigModal, DesignerRightPanel, settingsStyles.ts all have `outline: "none"`. |
74	| All | Cat 7 | Shared | "none use the 5 `--io-input-*` tokens" | **NO** | ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367 uses `var(--io-input-bg)` and `var(--io-input-border)` — but see next row for correction on "unregistered" claim. |
75	| Settings | Cat 7 | Visual properties | "`labelStyle`: 12px/500/`--io-text-secondary`/uppercase/0.05em" | **NO** | settingsStyles.ts:15-21: `labelStyle` has `fontSize: "12px"`, `fontWeight: 500`, `color: "var(--io-text-secondary)"`, `marginBottom: "5px"`. **No `textTransform: "uppercase"` and no `letterSpacing`**. Cat 9 for Settings correctly says "(no uppercase)"; Cat 7 is wrong. |
76	| Designer | Cat 7 | Deviations | "`--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border` in `ShapePointSelector` appear to be unregistered" | **NO** | Both tokens ARE registered: `--io-input-bg: var(--io-surface-sunken)` at index.css:131 and `--io-input-border: var(--io-border)` at index.css:132. ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367 uses them both. Not unregistered. |
77	| Settings | Cat 7 | Deviations | "`--io-space-*` tokens in `BulkUpdate`/`RestorePreviewModal` appear unregistered" | **NO** | `--io-space-0` through `--io-space-48` are defined in index.css:148-164 (17 tokens). Not unregistered. |
78	| All | Cat 7 | Shared | "none use the 5 `--io-input-*` tokens" | **NO** | Specifically `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border` ARE used in ShapePointSelector.tsx. Claim should read "Console and most of Settings use none; Designer's ShapePointSelector uses `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border`." |
79	
80	### Category 8 — Status Indicators
81	
82	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
83	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
84	| Console | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "Connection dot: 6×6px circle, `var(--io-success)`/`var(--io-warning)`/`var(--io-danger)`" | YES | console/index.tsx:76-84 confirmed. |
85	| Designer | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "WS dot: `●` glyph 8px, `#22c55e`/`#ef4444` hardcoded" | YES | DesignerStatusBar.tsx:208-213 `color: wsConnected ? "#22c55e" : "#ef4444"` at fontSize 8 with `●`. |
86	| Designer | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "dirty indicator: 7×7px div, `#f97316` hardcoded" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1585-1596 `width: 7, height: 7, background: "#f97316"`. |
87	| Designer | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "READ-ONLY badge: `#eab308`/`rgba(234,179,8,*)` hardcoded" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1600-1614 `background: "rgba(234,179,8,0.15)"`, `color: "#eab308"`. |
88	| Designer | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "modified tab dot: `var(--io-warning, #f59e0b)` — only token-using indicator" | YES | DesignerTabBar.tsx:185 `color: "var(--io-warning, #f59e0b)"` confirmed. |
89	| Designer | Cat 8 | Notes | "TEST MODE injects a new `<style>` element on every render cycle" | YES (partial) | DesignerStatusBar.tsx:316 injects `<style>` as JSX when `testMode` is true. React reconciliation re-uses the same DOM element on re-renders (not truly "new on every render"), but the `<style>` tag does exist inline in JSX and is created fresh on mount. Performance concern is valid; "every render" overstates it. |
90	| Settings | Cat 8 | Deviations | "`--io-info` undefined in `AuthProviders TypeBadge` (always fallback blue `#3b82f6`)" | **NO** | `--io-info` IS defined in index.css:72 as `#3b82f6`. AuthProviders.tsx:528 uses `"var(--io-info, #3b82f6)"` with a fallback that equals the token value. Token resolves correctly — not an undefined token. |
91	| All | Cat 8 | Deviations | "hardcoded `#22c55e` for connection dot glow shadows" | YES | Confirmed in OpcSources and SystemHealth. |
92	| Settings | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "`OpcSources StatusBadge`: hex-alpha concat bug (broken background)" | YES | OpcSources.tsx:157-168: `STATUS_COLORS` maps statuses to CSS variables (e.g., `"var(--io-success)"`). `background: \`${color}20\`` produces `"var(--io-success)20"` — invalid CSS. Bug confirmed. |
93	
94	### Category 9 — Labels and Headers
95	
96	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
97	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
98	| Designer | Cat 9 | Notes | "`FieldLabel` is consistently applied throughout `DesignerRightPanel`; semantically correct (`<label>` element)" | YES | DesignerRightPanel.tsx:201-203: `function FieldLabel` returns `<label>` element. Used at lines 240, 513, 542, 1837, 2274, 2301, 2366. |
99	| Settings | Cat 9 | Visual properties | "field label: 12px/500/`--io-text-secondary` (no uppercase)" | YES | Correct and consistent with actual settingsStyles.ts:15-21. This row in Cat 9 is accurate where Cat 7 is wrong. |
100	
101	### Category 10 — Canvas / Main Work Area
102	
103	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
104	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
105	| Designer | Cat 10 | Source-of-truth | "`DesignerCanvas.tsx:7452–12067`" | YES | DesignerCanvas.tsx is 12,067 lines; line 7452 is the return statement of the main component. |
106	| Designer | Cat 10 | Visual properties | "Container: `background: var(--io-surface-sunken)` (outside canvas bounds)" | YES | DesignerCanvas.tsx:7473 confirmed. |
107	| Designer | Cat 10 | Visual properties | "canvas background `<rect>`: live from `doc.canvas.backgroundColor` or `var(--io-surface-primary)` fallback" | YES | DesignerCanvas.tsx:7287 `const bgColor = doc?.canvas.backgroundColor ?? "var(--io-surface-primary)"` confirmed. |
108	| Designer | Cat 10 | Deviations | "selection resize handles `fill: 'white'` not token-adaptive" | YES | DesignerCanvas.tsx:1599, 1955, 2012, 2345 all show `fill="white"`. |
109	| Designer | Cat 10 | Deviations | "`--io-error` (context menu destructive item) is undefined — correct token is `--io-danger`" | YES | `--io-error` not in index.css. DesignerCanvas.tsx:8075, 10322 use `"var(--io-error, #ef4444)"` with fallback. |
110	| Designer | Cat 10 | Notes | "`io-multiselect-active` class is added to SVG but has no observable CSS rule" | **NO** | index.css:904-909 defines `svg.io-multiselect-active [data-node-id].io-selected { filter: drop-shadow(...) }`. The class DOES have a CSS rule. |
111	| Console | Cat 10 | Visual properties | "grid container background inherits `var(--io-bg)` (undefined token)" | YES | console/index.tsx:1878, 1938 use `background: "var(--io-bg)"`. Not in index.css. |
112	
113	### Category 11 — Modals and Dialogs
114	
115	| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
116	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
117	| Console | Cat 11 | Visual properties | "Inline modal backdrop: `rgba(0,0,0,0.5)`; z-index 9999; `PaneConfigModal` z-index 1001; Replace Graphic z-index 4000, `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)`" | YES | console/index.tsx:3465-3469 `rgba(0,0,0,0.5)` + zIndex 9999; PaneConfigModal.tsx:228 zIndex 1001; PaneWrapper.tsx:966-967 zIndex 4000 + `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)`. |
118	| Console | Cat 11 | Deviations | "3 inline modals lack `role='dialog'`/`aria-modal`" | YES | No `role="dialog"` or `aria-modal` in console/index.tsx inline modals. |
119	| Designer | Cat 11 | Deviations | "only `RecognitionWizard` has `role='dialog'`/`aria-modal`" | YES | RecognitionWizard.tsx:110-111 confirmed; no ARIA found in other designer dialog files checked. |
120	| Settings | Cat 11 | Visual properties | "Five distinct overlay specs: `var(--io-modal-backdrop)` (undefined), `var(--io-overlay, rgba(0,0,0,0.5))`…" | **NO** | `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined in index.css:139 as `var(--io-surface-overlay)`. Calling it "undefined" is incorrect. Email.tsx, RestorePreviewModal.tsx, and others correctly use a defined token. `--io-overlay` IS undefined (not in index.css) and has its fallback applied. |
121	| Settings | Cat 11 | Deviations | "4 native `window.confirm()` calls" | **NO** | Actual count: **8 calls** — OpcSources.tsx (3 calls: lines 585, 2947, 3009), Import.tsx (3 calls: lines 846, 907, 2722), CameraStreams.tsx (1 call: line 1004), SupplementalConnectorsTab.tsx (1 call: line 584). |
122	| Settings | Cat 11 | Notes | "`RestorePreviewModal` is the most urgent accessibility gap (destructive path, no ARIA)" | YES | RestorePreviewModal.tsx has no `role="dialog"` or `aria-modal`. Confirmed. |
123	
124	### List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules
125	
126	| # | Claim | Verified | Correction |
127	|---|---|---|---|
128	| 1 | "All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry." | YES | — |
129	| 2 | "All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens." | YES | — |
130	| 3 | "All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu." | YES | — |
131	| 4 | "All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons." | YES | — |
132	| 5 | "All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants." | YES | — |
133	| 6 | "All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-*` tokens." | YES | — |
134	| 7 | "All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-*` tokens." | **NO** | ShapePointSelector.tsx (Designer) uses `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border`. Should read "Console and Settings ignore all `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's ShapePointSelector uses two of them." |
135	| 8 | "All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) without a CSS replacement." | YES | — |
136	| 9 | "All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation for at least some hover states." | YES | — |
137	| 10 | "All three represent connection state with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error." | YES | — |
138	| 11 | "All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`." | **NO** | `--io-modal-backdrop` is used in Email.tsx, RestorePreviewModal.tsx, Recognition.tsx, ReportScheduling.tsx, SmsProviders.tsx, and EulaAdmin.tsx. `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used. The claim is wrong on both counts: it's neither ignored nor undefined. |
139	| 12 | "All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons." | YES | — |
140	
141	### List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules
142	
143	| # | Claim | Verified | Correction |
144	|---|---|---|---|
145	| 1 | Side panel background: Console/Settings `var(--io-surface-secondary)`; Designer left palette `var(--io-surface)`. | YES | — |
146	| 2 | Modal backdrop inconsistency across modules. | YES | — |
147	| 3 | Form input background: three different surface tiers across modules. | YES | — |
148	| 4 | Section/field label typography: three different size/weight/transform combinations. | YES | — |
149	| 5 | StatusBadge: five incompatible implementations in Settings. | YES | — |
150	| 6 | Modal ARIA gaps differ per module. | YES | — |
151	| 7 | "All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones… Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-info`, `--io-space-*`, `--io-accent-rgb`, `--io-surface`" | **NO** | Four of the seven tokens listed for Settings ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), and `--io-surface` (index.css:29). Only three are genuinely undefined: `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb`. |
152	| 8 | Button hover state differences across modules. | YES | — |
153	| 9 | Toolbar structure differences. | YES | — |
154	| 10 | Menu pattern consistency: Settings uses only shared ContextMenu; others add custom panels. | YES | — |
155	| 11 | Modal z-index values all uncoordinated. | YES | — |
156	| 12 | Semantic heading elements used inconsistently. | YES | — |
157	
158	### List 3 — Best version for convergence
159	
160	| # | Claim | Verified | Correction |
161	|---|---|---|---|
162	| 1 | Form input centralization — `settingsStyles.ts`. | YES | — |
163	| 2 | StatusBadge — `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern. | YES | — |
164	| 3 | Button system naming — `settingsStyles.ts`. | YES | — |
165	| 4 | Page-level label structure — `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`. | YES | — |
166	| 5 | Menu implementation discipline — Settings uses only shared ContextMenu. | YES | — |
167	| 6 | Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component. | YES | — |
168	| 7 | Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage. | YES | — |
169	
170	---
171	
172	## Discrepancy Summary
173	
174	11 discrepancies found in the comparison file. Each is a claim that the code contradicts:
175	
176	1. **Cat 1 Notes (Designer)** — SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS: claimed "fully hardcoded" but two entries reference `var(--io-accent)` and `var(--io-text-secondary)`. (`DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55`)
177	
178	2. **Cat 7 Visual Properties (Settings)** — `labelStyle`: claimed "uppercase/0.05em" but actual code has neither `textTransform: "uppercase"` nor `letterSpacing`. (`settingsStyles.ts:15-21`)
179	
180	3. **Cat 7 Deviations (Designer)** — `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border` in ShapePointSelector: claimed "appear to be unregistered." Both ARE registered in index.css (lines 131-132). (`ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367`)
181	
182	4. **Cat 7 Deviations (Settings)** — `--io-space-*`: claimed "appear unregistered." Seventeen `--io-space-*` tokens ARE defined in index.css:148-164.
183	
184	5. **Cat 8 Deviations (Settings)** — `--io-info` in AuthProviders TypeBadge: claimed "undefined." `--io-info: #3b82f6` IS defined in index.css:72. Fallback value equals token value; no regression exists. (`AuthProviders.tsx:528`)
185	
186	6. **Cat 10 Notes (Designer)** — `io-multiselect-active`: claimed "no observable CSS rule." Rule exists at index.css:904-909 (`svg.io-multiselect-active [data-node-id].io-selected { filter: drop-shadow(…) }`).
187	
188	7. **Cat 11 Deviations (Settings)** — native confirm() count: claimed "4 calls." Actual count is **8 calls** across 4 files: OpcSources(3), Import(3), CameraStreams(1), SupplementalConnectorsTab(1).
189	
190	8. **Cat 11 Visual Properties (Settings)** / **List 2 Item 7** — `--io-modal-backdrop`: claimed "undefined." `--io-modal-backdrop: var(--io-surface-overlay)` IS defined in index.css:139 and used in ≥6 Settings files.
191	
192	9. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-info`: claimed undefined. IS defined (see #5 above).
193	
194	10. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-space-*`: claimed undefined. IS defined (see #4 above).
195	
196	11. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-surface`: claimed undefined. `--io-surface: var(--io-surface-elevated)` IS defined in index.css:29.
197	
198	---
199	
200	## Missing-from-Comparison Entries
201	
202	4 elements exist in code but have no entry in the comparison:
203	
204	1. **`shared/clipboard/selection/selection.css` + `MarqueeLayer.tsx`** — Both reference `var(--accent)` (without the `--io-` prefix), a token not defined anywhere in the codebase. `selection.css` lines 2 and 9 use it for selection box outline (`2px solid var(--accent)`) and glow (`box-shadow: … var(--accent)`). `MarqueeLayer.tsx:101` uses `border: "1px dashed var(--accent)"`. With no definition, all three resolve to the CSS initial value, meaning selection highlight and marquee border have no visible color. Should be `var(--io-accent)`.
205	
206	2. **`shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css`** — CSS keyframe animations for alarm flash states (`io-alarm-flash-urgent`, `io-alarm-flash-high`, etc.) use hardcoded hex colors: `#ef4444`, `#f97316`, `#eab308`, `#f4f4f5`, `#60a5fa` rather than `--io-alarm-*` tokens. These animations apply to display elements in the graphics canvas but do not adapt to light or HPHMI themes. The comparison covers alarm badge colors in Cat 1 and 8 but does not mention this file.
207	
208	3. **`shared/graphics/operationalState.css`** — ISA-101 equipment operational state CSS classes (`io-running`, `io-stopped`, `io-fault`, `io-transitioning`, `io-oos`) apply hardcoded hex fills and strokes (`#047857`, `#808080`, `#dc2626`, `#d97706`) to SVG elements via `!important` overrides. No token system used. Not mentioned in the comparison under any category.
209	
210	4. **`shared/graphics/lod.css`** — Level-of-detail visibility rules for graphics elements. Structural CSS only (opacity transitions, `display: none` for non-adjacent LOD levels). No color tokens used. Not mentioned in the comparison; relevant to Canvas / Main Work Area (Cat 10) as a separate style layer governing display element visibility.
211	
212	---
213	
214	## Totals
215	
216	- **Rows checked:** 86 (55 table rows + 31 list items)
217	- **Discrepancies found:** 11
218	- **Missing-from-comparison entries:** 4
219

# UI Audit Verification — Code-Level Fact-Check

**Source verified against:** live frontend source code only. No design docs or audit files consulted.
**Date:** 2026-05-27

---

## Verification Method

Every claim in the 11 category tables (5 rows each × 11 categories = 55 table rows) and 3 lists (12 + 12 + 7 = 31 items) was checked by reading the cited source files. Source of truth is the code. Comparison file (`02-comparison.md`) is not amended.

---

## Table of Results

### Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| All | Cat 1 | Implementation | "all colors via CSS custom properties from `index.css`; no module-specific token overrides" | YES | — |
| All | Cat 1 | Source-of-truth files | "`index.css:19–295` (138 tokens)" | YES | The comment in index.css itself states 138 tokens; the range 19–295 reaches through the light-theme pen colors, which is correct for Console. Designer/Settings ranges (19–219 and 17–219) correspond to the dark-theme block end and the block opening comment; minor variation but not wrong. |
| All | Cat 1 | Visual properties | "~20 token references; hardcoded hex for alarm priority/quality badge colors, TrendPane series palette, published dot" | YES | Confirmed: `#10b981` published dot in console/index.tsx:2034; PRIORITY_COLOR hardcoded in AlarmListPane.tsx:34-35; QualityBadge hardcoded in PointTablePane.tsx:30-34. |
| Designer | Cat 1 | Notes | "SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS are **fully hardcoded** rgba objects with no registry equivalent" | **NO** | `SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"` (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48) and `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"` (line 55). Two of the six entries use registered tokens. Not "fully hardcoded." |
| All | Cat 1 | Deviations | "`var(--io-bg)` and `var(--io-text)` referenced but not defined in `index.css`" | YES | Confirmed: neither `--io-bg` nor bare `--io-text` appears in index.css. Both are genuinely undefined. |

### Category 2 — Typography

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| All | Cat 2 | Implementation | "All three use inline-styles exclusively; no shared typography component exists across modules" | YES | — |
| All | Cat 2 | Visual properties | "Raw px integers 9–18px; `fontFamily: monospace`" | YES | Groups.tsx:197 `fontFamily: "monospace"` confirmed. |
| All | Cat 2 | Deviations | "Zero typography scale tokens used" | YES | No file in any of the three modules references `--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`. |

### Category 3 — Toolbars

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Console | Cat 3 | Visual properties | "48px height; `background: var(--io-surface)`" | YES | console/index.tsx:1949-1951 confirmed. |
| Designer | Cat 3 | Visual properties | "DesignerToolbar 44px; mode/tab strips with 2px accent underline" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1162 `height: 44`; DesignerModeTabs.tsx:210 `height: 36`; DesignerTabBar.tsx:370 `height: 36`; DesignerStatusBar.tsx:178 `height: 28`. |
| Designer | Cat 3 | Notes | "Loading skeleton toolbar placeholder uses 40px height vs the actual 44px" | YES | designer/index.tsx:773 `height: 40` for skeleton toolbar row vs actual 44px. |
| Console | Cat 3 | Deviations | "Publish button rendered twice in right controls block (duplicate render)" | YES | console/index.tsx:2294-2325 and 2721-2752 both render the publish toggle button under `{canPublish && (...)`. |

### Category 4 — Menus

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| All | Cat 4 | Visual properties | "ContextMenu: `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger items rather than `var(--io-danger)`" | YES | shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx:247 `? "var(--io-alarm-urgent)"` confirmed. |
| Designer | Cat 4 | Notes | "File menu has no Escape key handler" | YES | No `Escape`/`keydown`/`onKeyDown` handler found in DesignerModeTabs.tsx File menu code. |
| Designer | Cat 4 | Deviations | "zoom dropdown hover uses `var(--io-surface-hover)` (undefined token)" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1459 `e.currentTarget.style.background = "var(--io-surface-hover)"` confirmed. `--io-surface-hover` not in index.css. |
| Designer | Cat 4 | Deviations | "zoom font uses `var(--io-font-sans)` (undefined)" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1456 `fontFamily: "var(--io-font-sans)"` confirmed. Not in index.css. |

### Category 5 — Side Panels

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Console | Cat 5 | Visual properties | "220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`" | YES | ConsolePalette.tsx uses `width: "220px"` style. |
| Settings | Cat 5 | Visual properties | "220px; `background: var(--io-surface-secondary)`" | YES | settings/index.tsx:182 `width: "220px"` confirmed. |
| All | Cat 5 | Deviations | "220px vs `--io-sidebar-width: 240px`" | YES | `--io-sidebar-width: 240px` at index.css:106; all three modules use hardcoded 220px. |
| Settings | Cat 5 | Deviations | "active left-border accent missing" | YES | No `borderLeft`, `--io-sidebar-active-border`, or `2px solid var(--io-accent)` found in settings/index.tsx nav items. |

### Category 6 — Buttons

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| All | Cat 6 | Shared | "none use the 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens" | YES | `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, etc. appear nowhere in console, designer, or settings page files. |
| Settings | Cat 6 | Visual properties | "`btnPrimary`: `var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-text-on-accent)` (undefined)" | YES | settingsStyles.ts:22-31 confirmed. `--io-text-on-accent` not in index.css. |
| Settings | Cat 6 | Notes | "`BulkUpdate.tsx` defines its own full button set (`BTN_PRIMARY`, `BTN_SECONDARY`, `BTN_DANGER`) independently from `settingsStyles`" | YES | BulkUpdate.tsx:33-63 confirmed. Uses `borderRadius: "6px"` string instead of `var(--io-radius)`. |

### Category 7 — Form Inputs

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| All | Cat 7 | Shared | "All suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) without a CSS replacement" | YES | ConsolePalette, PaneConfigModal, DesignerRightPanel, settingsStyles.ts all have `outline: "none"`. |
| All | Cat 7 | Shared | "none use the 5 `--io-input-*` tokens" | **NO** | ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367 uses `var(--io-input-bg)` and `var(--io-input-border)` — but see next row for correction on "unregistered" claim. |
| Settings | Cat 7 | Visual properties | "`labelStyle`: 12px/500/`--io-text-secondary`/uppercase/0.05em" | **NO** | settingsStyles.ts:15-21: `labelStyle` has `fontSize: "12px"`, `fontWeight: 500`, `color: "var(--io-text-secondary)"`, `marginBottom: "5px"`. **No `textTransform: "uppercase"` and no `letterSpacing`**. Cat 9 for Settings correctly says "(no uppercase)"; Cat 7 is wrong. |
| Designer | Cat 7 | Deviations | "`--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border` in `ShapePointSelector` appear to be unregistered" | **NO** | Both tokens ARE registered: `--io-input-bg: var(--io-surface-sunken)` at index.css:131 and `--io-input-border: var(--io-border)` at index.css:132. ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367 uses them both. Not unregistered. |
| Settings | Cat 7 | Deviations | "`--io-space-*` tokens in `BulkUpdate`/`RestorePreviewModal` appear unregistered" | **NO** | `--io-space-0` through `--io-space-48` are defined in index.css:148-164 (17 tokens). Not unregistered. |
| All | Cat 7 | Shared | "none use the 5 `--io-input-*` tokens" | **NO** | Specifically `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border` ARE used in ShapePointSelector.tsx. Claim should read "Console and most of Settings use none; Designer's ShapePointSelector uses `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border`." |

### Category 8 — Status Indicators

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Console | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "Connection dot: 6×6px circle, `var(--io-success)`/`var(--io-warning)`/`var(--io-danger)`" | YES | console/index.tsx:76-84 confirmed. |
| Designer | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "WS dot: `●` glyph 8px, `#22c55e`/`#ef4444` hardcoded" | YES | DesignerStatusBar.tsx:208-213 `color: wsConnected ? "#22c55e" : "#ef4444"` at fontSize 8 with `●`. |
| Designer | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "dirty indicator: 7×7px div, `#f97316` hardcoded" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1585-1596 `width: 7, height: 7, background: "#f97316"`. |
| Designer | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "READ-ONLY badge: `#eab308`/`rgba(234,179,8,*)` hardcoded" | YES | DesignerToolbar.tsx:1600-1614 `background: "rgba(234,179,8,0.15)"`, `color: "#eab308"`. |
| Designer | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "modified tab dot: `var(--io-warning, #f59e0b)` — only token-using indicator" | YES | DesignerTabBar.tsx:185 `color: "var(--io-warning, #f59e0b)"` confirmed. |
| Designer | Cat 8 | Notes | "TEST MODE injects a new `<style>` element on every render cycle" | YES (partial) | DesignerStatusBar.tsx:316 injects `<style>` as JSX when `testMode` is true. React reconciliation re-uses the same DOM element on re-renders (not truly "new on every render"), but the `<style>` tag does exist inline in JSX and is created fresh on mount. Performance concern is valid; "every render" overstates it. |
| Settings | Cat 8 | Deviations | "`--io-info` undefined in `AuthProviders TypeBadge` (always fallback blue `#3b82f6`)" | **NO** | `--io-info` IS defined in index.css:72 as `#3b82f6`. AuthProviders.tsx:528 uses `"var(--io-info, #3b82f6)"` with a fallback that equals the token value. Token resolves correctly — not an undefined token. |
| All | Cat 8 | Deviations | "hardcoded `#22c55e` for connection dot glow shadows" | YES | Confirmed in OpcSources and SystemHealth. |
| Settings | Cat 8 | Visual properties | "`OpcSources StatusBadge`: hex-alpha concat bug (broken background)" | YES | OpcSources.tsx:157-168: `STATUS_COLORS` maps statuses to CSS variables (e.g., `"var(--io-success)"`). `background: \`${color}20\`` produces `"var(--io-success)20"` — invalid CSS. Bug confirmed. |

### Category 9 — Labels and Headers

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Designer | Cat 9 | Notes | "`FieldLabel` is consistently applied throughout `DesignerRightPanel`; semantically correct (`<label>` element)" | YES | DesignerRightPanel.tsx:201-203: `function FieldLabel` returns `<label>` element. Used at lines 240, 513, 542, 1837, 2274, 2301, 2366. |
| Settings | Cat 9 | Visual properties | "field label: 12px/500/`--io-text-secondary` (no uppercase)" | YES | Correct and consistent with actual settingsStyles.ts:15-21. This row in Cat 9 is accurate where Cat 7 is wrong. |

### Category 10 — Canvas / Main Work Area

| Designer | Cat 10 | Deviations | "selection resize handles `fill: 'white'` not token-adaptive" | YES | DesignerCanvas.tsx:1599, 1955, 2012, 2345 all show `fill="white"`. |
| Designer | Cat 10 | Deviations | "`--io-error` (context menu destructive item) is undefined — correct token is `--io-danger`" | YES | `--io-error` not in index.css. DesignerCanvas.tsx:8075, 10322 use `"var(--io-error, #ef4444)"` with fallback. |
| Designer | Cat 10 | Notes | "`io-multiselect-active` class is added to SVG but has no observable CSS rule" | **NO** | index.css:904-909 defines `svg.io-multiselect-active [data-node-id].io-selected { filter: drop-shadow(...) }`. The class DOES have a CSS rule. |
| Console | Cat 10 | Visual properties | "grid container background inherits `var(--io-bg)` (undefined token)" | YES | console/index.tsx:1878, 1938 use `background: "var(--io-bg)"`. Not in index.css. |

### Category 11 — Modals and Dialogs

| Module | Category | Field | Claim as stated | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Console | Cat 11 | Visual properties | "Inline modal backdrop: `rgba(0,0,0,0.5)`; z-index 9999; `PaneConfigModal` z-index 1001; Replace Graphic z-index 4000, `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)`" | YES | console/index.tsx:3465-3469 `rgba(0,0,0,0.5)` + zIndex 9999; PaneConfigModal.tsx:228 zIndex 1001; PaneWrapper.tsx:966-967 zIndex 4000 + `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)`. |
| Console | Cat 11 | Deviations | "3 inline modals lack `role='dialog'`/`aria-modal`" | YES | No `role="dialog"` or `aria-modal` in console/index.tsx inline modals. |
| Designer | Cat 11 | Deviations | "only `RecognitionWizard` has `role='dialog'`/`aria-modal`" | YES | RecognitionWizard.tsx:110-111 confirmed; no ARIA found in other designer dialog files checked. |

Calling it "undefined" is incorrect. Email.tsx, RestorePreviewModal.tsx, and others correctly use a defined token. `--io-overlay` IS undefined (not in index.css) and has its fallback applied. |
| Settings | Cat 11 | Deviations | "4 native `window.confirm()` calls" | **NO** | Actual count: **8 calls** — OpcSources.tsx (3 calls: lines 585, 2947, 3009), Import.tsx (3 calls: lines 846, 907, 2722), CameraStreams.tsx (1 call: line 1004), SupplementalConnectorsTab.tsx (1 call: line 584). |
| Settings | Cat 11 | Notes | "`RestorePreviewModal` is the most urgent accessibility gap (destructive path, no ARIA)" | YES | RestorePreviewModal.tsx has no `role="dialog"` or `aria-modal`. Confirmed. |

### List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules

| # | Claim | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry." | YES | — |
| 2 | "All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens." | YES | — |
| 3 | "All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu." | YES | — |
| 4 | "All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons." | YES | — |
| 5 | "All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants." | YES | — |
| 6 | "All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-*` tokens." | YES | — |

| 8 | "All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) without a CSS replacement." | YES | — |
| 9 | "All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation for at least some hover states." | YES | — |
| 10 | "All three represent connection state with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error." | YES | — |
| 11 | "All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`." | **NO** | `--io-modal-backdrop` is used in Email.tsx, RestorePreviewModal.tsx, Recognition.tsx, ReportScheduling.tsx, SmsProviders.tsx, and EulaAdmin.tsx. `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used. The claim is wrong on both counts: it's neither ignored nor undefined. |
| 12 | "All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons." | YES | — |

### List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules

| # | Claim | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Side panel background: Console/Settings `var(--io-surface-secondary)`; Designer left palette `var(--io-surface)`. | YES | — |
| 2 | Modal backdrop inconsistency across modules. | YES | — |
| 3 | Form input background: three different surface tiers across modules. | YES | — |
| 4 | Section/field label typography: three different size/weight/transform combinations. | YES | — |

| 7 | "All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones… Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-info`, `--io-space-*`, `--io-accent-rgb`, `--io-surface`" | **NO** | Four of the seven tokens listed for Settings ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), and `--io-surface` (index.css:29). Only three are genuinely undefined: `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb`. |
| 8 | Button hover state differences across modules. | YES | — |
| 9 | Toolbar structure differences. | YES | — |

| 12 | Semantic heading elements used inconsistently. | YES | — |

### List 3 — Best version for convergence

| # | Claim | Verified | Correction |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Form input centralization — `settingsStyles.ts`. | YES | — |
| 2 | StatusBadge — `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern. | YES | — |
| 3 | Button system naming — `settingsStyles.ts`. | YES | — |
| 4 | Page-level label structure — `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`. | YES | — |
| 5 | Menu implementation discipline — Settings uses only shared ContextMenu. | YES | — |
| 6 | Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component. | YES | — |
| 7 | Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage. | YES | — |

---

## Discrepancy Summary

11 discrepancies found in the comparison file. Each is a claim that the code contradicts:

1. **Cat 1 Notes (Designer)** — SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS: claimed "fully hardcoded" but two entries reference `var(--io-accent)` and `var(--io-text-secondary)`. (`DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55`)

2. **Cat 7 Visual Properties (Settings)** — `labelStyle`: claimed "uppercase/0.05em" but actual code has neither `textTransform: "uppercase"` nor `letterSpacing`. (`settingsStyles.ts:15-21`)

3. **Cat 7 Deviations (Designer)** — `--io-input-bg` and `--io-input-border` in ShapePointSelector: claimed "appear to be unregistered." Both ARE registered in index.css (lines 131-132). (`ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367`)

4. **Cat 7 Deviations (Settings)** — `--io-space-*`: claimed "appear unregistered." Seventeen `--io-space-*` tokens ARE defined in index.css:148-164.

5. **Cat 8 Deviations (Settings)** — `--io-info` in AuthProviders TypeBadge: claimed "undefined." `--io-info: #3b82f6` IS defined in index.css:72. Fallback value equals token value; no regression exists. (`AuthProviders.tsx:528`)

6. **Cat 10 Notes (Designer)** — `io-multiselect-active`: claimed "no observable CSS rule." Rule exists at index.css:904-909 (`svg.io-multiselect-active [data-node-id].io-selected { filter: drop-shadow(…) }`).

7. **Cat 11 Deviations (Settings)** — native confirm() count: claimed "4 calls." Actual count is **8 calls** across 4 files: OpcSources(3), Import(3), CameraStreams(1), SupplementalConnectorsTab(1).

8. **Cat 11 Visual Properties (Settings)** / **List 2 Item 7** — `--io-modal-backdrop`: claimed "undefined." `--io-modal-backdrop: var(--io-surface-overlay)` IS defined in index.css:139 and used in ≥6 Settings files.

9. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-info`: claimed undefined. IS defined (see #5 above).

10. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-space-*`: claimed undefined. IS defined (see #4 above).

11. **List 2 Item 7 (Settings)** — `--io-surface`: claimed undefined. `--io-surface: var(--io-surface-elevated)` IS defined in index.css:29.

---

## Missing-from-Comparison Entries

4 elements exist in code but have no entry in the comparison:

1. **`shared/clipboard/selection/selection.css` + `MarqueeLayer.tsx`** — Both reference `var(--accent)` (without the `--io-` prefix), a token not defined anywhere in the codebase. `selection.css` lines 2 and 9 use it for selection box outline (`2px solid var(--accent)`) and glow (`box-shadow: … var(--accent)`). `MarqueeLayer.tsx:101` uses `border: "1px dashed var(--accent)"`. With no definition, all three resolve to the CSS initial value, meaning selection highlight and marquee border have no visible color. Should be `var(--io-accent)`.

2. **`shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css`** — CSS keyframe animations for alarm flash states (`io-alarm-flash-urgent`, `io-alarm-flash-high`, etc.) use hardcoded hex colors: `#ef4444`, `#f97316`, `#eab308`, `#f4f4f5`, `#60a5fa` rather than `--io-alarm-*` tokens. These animations apply to display elements in the graphics canvas but do not adapt to light or HPHMI themes. The comparison covers alarm badge colors in Cat 1 and 8 but does not mention this file.

3. **`shared/graphics/operationalState.css`** — ISA-101 equipment operational state CSS classes (`io-running`, `io-stopped`, `io-fault`, `io-transitioning`, `io-oos`) apply hardcoded hex fills and strokes (`#047857`, `#808080`, `#dc2626`, `#d97706`) to SVG elements via `!important` overrides. No token system used. Not mentioned in the comparison under any category.

4. **`shared/graphics/lod.css`** — Level-of-detail visibility rules for graphics elements. Structural CSS only (opacity transitions, `display: none` for non-adjacent LOD levels). No color tokens used. Not mentioned in the comparison; relevant to Canvas / Main Work Area (Cat 10) as a separate style layer governing display element visibility.

---

## Totals

- **Rows checked:** 86 (55 table rows + 31 list items)
- **Discrepancies found:** 11
- **Missing-from-comparison entries:** 4

113	
114	| Field | alarmFlash.css |
115	|---|---|
116	| **Implementation** | CSS `@keyframes` animations and class selectors for alarm flash states on SVG display elements in the graphics canvas. Ten keyframe animations (5 alarm levels × 2: stroke-based for shapes, fill-based for text elements) plus one box-flash animation for text readout components. All at `1s steps(1) infinite`. Applied by SceneRenderer as class assignments on symbol instance `<g>` wrappers. |
117	| **Source-of-truth files** | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css:1-149` |
118	| **Visual properties actually applied** | `io-alarm-flash-urgent` (stroke): `#ef4444` active / `#808080` off; `io-alarm-flash-high`: `#f97316` / `#808080`; `io-alarm-flash-low`: `#eab308` / `#808080`; `io-alarm-flash-diagnostic`: `#f4f4f5` / `#808080`; `io-alarm-flash-custom`: `#60a5fa` / `#808080`; `io-alarm-box-flash` off-state: `fill: #27272a, stroke: #3f3f46`. `> *` selector for stroke, `text` for fill, `rect` for box background. |
119	| **Deviations from app shell** | All alarm colors are hardcoded hex; token equivalents exist: `--io-alarm-urgent: #ef4444` (index.css:51), `--io-alarm-high: #f97316` (52), `--io-alarm-low: #eab308` (53), `--io-alarm-diagnostic: #f4f4f5` (54), `--io-alarm-custom: #60a5fa` (55). Off-state `#808080` has no token equivalent. `io-alarm-box-flash` off-state hardcodes dark-theme hex values for `--io-surface-elevated` and `--io-border` without token references. Does not adapt to light or HPHMI themes. |
120	| **Notes** | Hardcoded values match dark-theme token values exactly; visual output is correct in dark theme. Light/HPHMI adaptation would be broken. Not covered in per-module Cat 1 or Cat 8 entries. Primary: Cat 8. Secondary: Cat 10. |
121	
122	| Field | operationalState.css |
123	|---|---|
124	| **Implementation** | ISA-101 operational state CSS classes applied as class names to symbol `<g>` wrapper elements. Five mutually exclusive states: `io-running`, `io-stopped`, `io-fault`, `io-transitioning`, `io-oos`. Uses `!important` overrides to guarantee state classes override shape-specific SVG attributes set at authoring time. One `@keyframes` animation (`io-state-transitioning-pulse`) and one `@supports` OOS hatch fallback. |
125	| **Source-of-truth files** | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/operationalState.css:1-73` |
126	| **Visual properties actually applied** | `io-running`: stroke `#047857 !important` (emerald-700), circle fill `rgba(5,150,105,0.15) !important`; `io-stopped`: stroke `#808080 !important`; `io-fault`: stroke `#dc2626 !important`, circle fill `rgba(220,38,38,0.12) !important`; `io-transitioning`: stroke `#d97706 !important` + `opacity 1→0.5→1, 1.5s ease-in-out infinite` pulse; `io-oos`: stroke `#808080 !important` + hatch fill |
127	| **Deviations from app shell** | All colors hardcoded hex with `!important` overrides. **Documented exception — intentional design, not a gap to close:** `!important` is required to override SVG attribute-level fill/stroke set in authored symbol geometry. ISA-101 prescribes specific operational state colors that intentionally differ from project tokens (e.g., `#047857` emerald-700 running vs `--io-alarm-normal: #22c55e` green-500 — different by standard; `#dc2626` fault vs `--io-alarm-urgent: #ef4444` — physically distinct semantic, different value by design). This file is intentionally non-token-driven for ISA-101 compliance. |
128	| **Notes** | The hardcoded ISA-101 colors with `!important` are the correct and intentional implementation. Do not treat as a gap to close in the recommendations phase. Consumed by SceneRenderer assigning state classes based on bound point operational state values. Primary: Cat 10. Secondary: Cat 8. |

197	| **Deviations from app shell** | `borderRadius: 8` vs `--io-modal-radius: var(--io-radius-lg)` = 9px; `var(--io-surface)` bg vs `--io-modal-bg: var(--io-surface-elevated)`; z-index values (9999, 1001, 4000) unrelated to `--io-z-modal: 300`; 3 inline modals lack `role="dialog"`/`aria-modal`; `var(--io-text)` undefined in dialog titles | No `--io-modal-*` tokens used; backdrop colors four distinct hardcoded values; z-index 1000–3000 uncoordinated; primary button text `#09090b` or `#fff` hardcoded (should be `var(--io-accent-foreground)`); error colors mix hardcoded hex, `var(--io-alarm-high)` (wrong domain), `var(--io-error)` (undefined); step indicator patterns inconsistent across 5 wizard dialogs; only `RecognitionWizard` has `role="dialog"`/`aria-modal` | Most widespread token deviations: `--io-overlay` is undefined; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139 as `var(--io-surface-overlay)`) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files (Email.tsx, RestorePreviewModal.tsx, and others); 8 native `window.confirm()` calls — OpcSources.tsx (3), Import.tsx (3), CameraStreams.tsx (1), SupplementalConnectorsTab.tsx (1); `RestorePreviewModal` has no ARIA on a destructive-adjacent action; `Import` `Modal`/`Drawer` have no ARIA | All three: z-index values uncoordinated with `--io-z-modal: 300`; all three have at least some plain-div modals with missing ARIA | — |
198	| **Notes** | `VersionRecoveryDialog` and `SaveConfirmDialog` are app-shell shared components; Console code adds no styles | All designer dialogs use `position: fixed` correctly (escapes canvas CSS transform context); `CanvasPropertiesDialog` is a floating non-modal panel; `VersionHistoryDialog` is a 36-line wrapper delegating to app-shell | `BulkUpdate` is the best modal-usage example in Settings: uses shared `ConfirmDialog` throughout, introduces no custom overlay patterns; `Certificates` demonstrates correct ARIA for plain-div modals; `RestorePreviewModal` is the most urgent accessibility gap (destructive path, no ARIA) | `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are not used in any module; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and used in ≥6 Settings files; Console and Designer do not use it; all three have inconsistent z-index layering with no reference to `--io-z-modal`; all three have at least some plain-div modals with missing ARIA | — |
199	
200	---
201	
202	## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules
203	
204	1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
205	2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
206	3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
207	4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
208	5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
209	6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
210	7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.
211	8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
212	9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
213	10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
214	11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.
215	12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.
216	
217	---
218	
219	## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules
220	
221	1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
222	2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
223	3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
224	4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
225	5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
226	6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
227	7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29).
228	8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
229	9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
230	10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
231	11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — all uncoordinated with the `--io-z-modal: 300` token.
232	12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.
233	
234	---
235	
236	## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on
237	
238	1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.
239	
240	2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.
241	
242	3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.
243	
244	4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.
245	
246	5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.
247	
248	6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.
249	
250	7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.
251	
252	---
253	
254	## Reconciliation Log
255	
256	*Per-module audit files (01-console.md, 01-designer.md, 01-settings.md) not updated; 02-comparison.md is the authoritative source post-reconciliation. All corrections below apply to 02-comparison.md only.*
257	
258	### Original Discrepancies — 03-verification.md
259	
260	1. **Cat 1, Notes, Designer** — Removed "fully hardcoded" claim for SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS; two of six entries use registered tokens (`SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"`, `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"`). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #1 (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55).
261	
262	2. **Cat 7, Visual Properties, Settings** — Removed "uppercase/0.05em" from labelStyle description; settingsStyles.ts:15-21 has neither `textTransform: "uppercase"` nor `letterSpacing`. Replaced with `marginBottom: 5px` and file citation. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #2.
263	
264	3. **Cat 7, Visual Properties, Designer** — Removed "(unregistered token)" from `--io-input-bg`; added `--io-input-border` entry with registration citations (index.css:131-132). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 (ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367).
265	
266	4. **Cat 7, Deviations, Designer** — Replaced "appear to be unregistered" for `--io-input-bg`/`--io-input-border` with confirmed-registered status and line references (index.css:131-132). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3.
267	
268	5. **Cat 7, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-space-*` appear unregistered" with confirmation that 17 tokens are defined at index.css:148-164. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #4.
269	
270	6. **Cat 7, Implementation, Shared column** — Changed "none use the 5 `--io-input-*` tokens" to note that Designer's ShapePointSelector uses two of the five (`--io-input-bg`, `--io-input-border`). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / List 1 Item 7 correction.
271	
272	7. **Cat 7, Deviations, Shared column** — Changed "All three: none use `--io-input-*` tokens" to accurate per-module split (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / Cat 7 table rows 4 and 8.
273	
274	8. **Cat 8, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-info` undefined" with confirmation that `--io-info: #3b82f6` is defined at index.css:72; fallback equals token value, no visual regression. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #5 (AuthProviders.tsx:528).
275	
276	9. **Cat 10, Notes, Designer** — Replaced "`io-multiselect-active` has no observable CSS rule" with confirmation that rule exists at index.css:904-909 (drop-shadow filter on selected nodes during multiselect). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #6.
277	
278	10. **Cat 11, Visual Properties, Settings** — Changed `--io-modal-backdrop` label from "(undefined)" to "(defined: `var(--io-surface-overlay)` at index.css:139)". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8.
279	
280	11. **Cat 11, Deviations, Settings** — Removed false "undefined" claim for `--io-modal-backdrop`; noted it is defined and used in ≥6 Settings files; corrected `window.confirm()` count from 4 to 8 with file breakdown (OpcSources:3, Import:3, CameraStreams:1, SupplementalConnectorsTab:1). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #7 and #8.
281	
282	12. **Cat 11, Notes, Shared column** — Changed "All three: `--io-modal-backdrop` and `--io-modal-bg` tokens are not used" to accurately note `--io-modal-backdrop` IS used in ≥6 Settings files; only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8 / List 1 Item 11 correction.
283	
284	13. **Cat 11, Implementation, Settings** — Updated `window.confirm()` count in implementation row from "4" to "8". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #7.
285	
286	14. **List 1, Item 7** — Changed "All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-*` tokens" to partial-use statement (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 7 correction.
287	
288	15. **List 1, Item 11** — Changed "All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`" to accurate statement: only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 11 correction.
289	
290	16. **List 2, Item 7, Settings undefined tokens** — Removed four tokens from Settings undefined list that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (discrepancy #8, index.css:139), `--io-info` (discrepancy #9, index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (discrepancy #10, index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (discrepancy #11, index.css:29). Retained genuinely undefined: `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #8–#11.
291	
292	### Additional Discrepancies — 03c-pre-reconciliation.md, Section 3
293	
294	17. **03c Discrepancy A — `--io-surface-sunken`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:26. Reviewed 02-comparison.md — no hedged language about `--io-surface-sunken` was present in the comparison file; it was already treated as a valid token. No text change to 02. The underlying inconsistency finding (Cat 5, Deviations, Designer: equipment tiles use `--io-surface-sunken` vs `--io-surface-elevated` for other tiles) remains a valid real inconsistency in surface-tier choice. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy A.
295	
296	18. **03c Discrepancy B — `--io-accent-subtle`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:42 (`rgba(45,212,191,0.1)`). Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-accent-subtle` does not appear in the comparison file; hedged claims were only in 01-designer.md. No text change to 02. The CanvasLayerRow indigo fallback (`rgba(99,102,241,0.1)`) is dead code with a wrong hue — latent but not breaking. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy B.
297	
298	19. **03c Discrepancy C — `--io-border-subtle`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:46 (`#27272a`). Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-border-subtle` appears in Cat 1 Visual Properties (Settings) already treated as a valid token reference. No hedged language present. No text change to 02. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy C.
299	
300	### Shared Infrastructure Additions — 03c Section 2 + 03-verification.md Missing-from-Comparison
301	
302	20. **Category 5 Shared Infrastructure — selection.css + MarqueeLayer.tsx (full entry)**: Added five-field entry covering the `var(--accent)` prefix bug (undefined token; should be `var(--io-accent)`), invisible selection overlay, and wrong hardcoded marquee background color (`rgba(80,180,255,0.08)` vs `var(--io-accent-subtle)`). Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #1; 03c Sections 2.1 and 2.2.
303	
304	21. **Category 8 Shared Infrastructure — alarmFlash.css (full entry)**: Added five-field entry covering hardcoded alarm hex colors (values match dark-theme tokens but do not adapt to light/HPHMI themes). Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #2; 03c Section 2.3.
305	
306	22. **Category 8 Shared Infrastructure — operationalState.css (full entry)**: Added five-field entry documenting intentional ISA-101 hardcoded colors with `!important` as a documented design exception — not a gap to close. Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #3; 03c Section 2.4.
307	
308	23. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — selection.css + MarqueeLayer.tsx (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 5). Sources: same as #20; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 secondary.
309	
310	24. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — alarmFlash.css (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 8). Sources: same as #21; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 secondary.
311	
312	25. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — operationalState.css (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 8). Sources: same as #22; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 primary, Cat 8 secondary.
313	
314	26. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — lod.css (full entry)**: Added five-field entry for structural-only CSS with no deviations; recorded for completeness per instruction. Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #4; 03c Section 2.5 (category assignment: Cat 10 exclusively).
315	
316	### Meta
317	
318	27. **Per-module audit files not updated**: 01-console.md, 01-designer.md, and 01-settings.md retain their original (some incorrect) content. 02-comparison.md is the authoritative source post-reconciliation. All corrections in this log apply to 02-comparison.md only.
