1	#!/bin/bash
2	# update-docs.sh — Update interim documentation based on work unit changes
3	#
4	# Usage:
5	#   update-docs.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID           # auto-detect affected docs
6	#   update-docs.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID SLUG      # update specific doc by slug
7	#
8	# The auto-detect mode:
9	#   1. Identifies files modified in this work unit
10	#   2. Finds existing interim docs whose `implementation:` field references
11	#      any of those files
12	#   3. Updates those docs to reflect the new behavior
13	#   4. If no existing doc covers a modified file, creates a new interim doc
14	#
15	# The targeted mode (with SLUG):
16	#   1. Reads the existing doc at .claude/docs/interim/SLUG.md
17	#   2. Updates it based on recent work unit content
18	
19	set -uo pipefail
20	
21	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
22	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
23	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
24	    source "$LIB_PATH"
25	fi
26	
27	LOG_PATH="$1"
28	SESSION_ID="$2"
29	TARGET_SLUG="${3:-}"
30	
31	if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
32	    echo "update-docs: log not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
33	    exit 1
34	fi
35	
36	ensure_workflow_dirs
37	
38	LOG_FILENAME=$(basename "$LOG_PATH" .md)
39	LOG_CONTENT=$(cat "$LOG_PATH")
40	TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
41	
42	# Try to find the corresponding summary
43	SUMMARY_PATH="${WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_summary.md"
44	SUMMARY_CONTENT=""
45	if [ -f "$SUMMARY_PATH" ]; then
46	    SUMMARY_CONTENT="$(cat "$SUMMARY_PATH")"
47	fi
48	
49	# ============================================================================
50	# Identify modified files
51	# ============================================================================
52	
53	FILES_MODIFIED=$(grep -oE '#(WRITE|EDIT|MULTIEDIT) `[^`]+`' "$LOG_PATH" | grep -oE '`[^`]+`' | tr -d '`' | sort -u)
54	
55	if [ -z "$FILES_MODIFIED" ] && [ -z "$TARGET_SLUG" ]; then
56	    echo "update-docs: no files modified and no target slug; nothing to do"
57	    exit 0
58	fi
59	
60	# ============================================================================
61	# Identify affected docs
62	# ============================================================================
63	
64	AFFECTED_DOCS=()
65	
66	if [ -n "$TARGET_SLUG" ]; then
67	    TARGET_DOC="${WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR}/${TARGET_SLUG}.md"
68	    if [ -f "$TARGET_DOC" ]; then
69	        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$TARGET_DOC")
70	    else
71	        # Doc doesn't exist; we'll create it below
72	        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$TARGET_DOC")
73	    fi
74	else
75	    # Auto-detect: scan existing interim docs for implementation: entries matching files
76	    if [ -d "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR" ]; then
77	        while IFS= read -r f; do
78	            if grep -qF -- "$f" "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null; then
79	                while IFS= read -r doc; do
80	                    # Check if this doc actually references this file in its implementation: section
81	                    if grep -A 20 'implementation:' "$doc" 2>/dev/null | grep -qF -- "$f"; then
82	                        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$doc")
83	                    fi
84	                done < <(grep -lF -- "$f" "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null)
85	            fi
86	        done <<< "$FILES_MODIFIED"
87	    fi
88	
89	    # Deduplicate
90	    if [ ${#AFFECTED_DOCS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
91	        readarray -t AFFECTED_DOCS < <(printf '%s\n' "${AFFECTED_DOCS[@]}" | sort -u)
92	    fi
93	fi
94	
95	# ============================================================================
96	# Update each affected doc
97	# ============================================================================
98	
99	if [ ${#AFFECTED_DOCS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
100	    # No existing docs cover the modified files. Create a new interim doc.
101	    # We'll let claude -p figure out an appropriate slug from the work unit.
102	
103	    NEW_SLUG_PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
104	Based on this work unit summary and log, suggest a short kebab-case slug
105	(3-6 words, lowercase, hyphen-separated) for an interim documentation file
106	covering the feature or area this work unit touched.
107	
108	Output ONLY the slug, nothing else. No quotes, no explanation.
109	
110	Summary:
111	$SUMMARY_CONTENT
112	
113	Log:
114	$LOG_CONTENT
115	EOF
116	)
117	
118	    NEW_SLUG=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$NEW_SLUG_PROMPT" 2>&1 | tr -d ' \n' | head -c 60)
119	    if [ -z "$NEW_SLUG" ] || ! echo "$NEW_SLUG" | grep -qE '^[a-z0-9-]+$'; then
120	        # Fallback to a generic slug based on the log filename
121	        NEW_SLUG=$(slugify "$LOG_FILENAME" | head -c 50)
122	    fi
123	
124	    NEW_DOC="${WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR}/${NEW_SLUG}.md"
125	    AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$NEW_DOC")
126	fi
127	
128	for doc_path in "${AFFECTED_DOCS[@]}"; do
129	    SLUG=$(basename "$doc_path" .md)
130	
131	    if [ -f "$doc_path" ]; then
132	        EXISTING_DOC=$(cat "$doc_path")
133	        MODE="update"
134	    else
135	        EXISTING_DOC=""
136	        MODE="create"
137	    fi
138	
139	    PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
140	You are maintaining an interim design document for a Claude Code project.
141	
142	Mode: $MODE
143	Document slug: $SLUG
144	Document path: $doc_path
145	
146	This is an INTERIM doc (status: interim) under .claude/docs/interim/. It will
147	eventually be merged into a canonical structure. For now, keep it focused on
148	describing CURRENT REALITY of the code.
149	
150	Schema for interim docs (frontmatter at top of file, in YAML between --- lines):
151	
152	  id: $SLUG
153	  title: <human-readable title>
154	  status: interim
155	  created: <YYYY-MM-DD, set once on creation>
156	  last_updated: <YYYY-MM-DD, today's date when updating>
157	  last_synced_with_code: <YYYY-MM-DD, today's date>
158	  work_units:
159	    - <list of work-unit log filenames that contributed>
160	  implementation:
161	    - <list of code file paths this doc describes>
162	  related:
163	    - <informal list of related doc slugs>
164	
165	Body sections (in this order):
166	  # <Title>
167	  <1-3 sentence summary>
168	
169	  ## Purpose
170	  <what this feature/area does>
171	
172	  ## Behavior
173	  <how it behaves, including caveats>
174	
175	  ## Implementation Notes
176	  <where the code lives, key design decisions>
177	
178	  ## Changelog
179	  <!-- IGNORE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ASKED TO REVIEW DOCUMENT HISTORY -->
180	  ### YYYY-MM-DD
181	  <Description of changes made on this date. Most recent on top.>
182	
183	YOUR JOB:
184	$(if [ "$MODE" = "create" ]; then
185	echo "- This doc does NOT yet exist. Create it fresh based on the work unit log and summary."
186	echo "- Set 'created' and 'last_updated' and 'last_synced_with_code' all to: $TODAY"
187	echo "- Populate work_units with: $LOG_FILENAME"
188	echo "- Populate implementation with the file paths actually modified in this work unit"
189	echo "- Add an initial changelog entry dated $TODAY describing the creation"
190	else
191	echo "- This doc EXISTS. Update it to reflect new behavior from this work unit."
192	echo "- Preserve the existing 'created' date (do NOT change it)"
193	echo "- Update 'last_updated' and 'last_synced_with_code' to: $TODAY"
194	echo "- APPEND the current work-unit log filename to work_units (if not already there): $LOG_FILENAME"
195	echo "- UPDATE implementation list if new files were touched"
196	echo "- UPDATE body sections to reflect new behavior (overwrite, don't append, in body sections)"
197	echo "- ADD a new changelog entry at the TOP of the Changelog section dated $TODAY, describing what changed"
198	echo "- KEEP previous changelog entries in place beneath the new one"
199	fi)
200	
201	OUTPUT RULES:
202	- Output ONLY the complete file content (frontmatter + body), nothing else
203	- Start with the opening --- of frontmatter
204	- Do NOT include preamble, explanation, or markdown code fences around the output
205	- The output replaces the entire file contents
206	
207	WORK UNIT LOG:
208	---
209	$LOG_CONTENT
210	---
211	
212	$([ -n "$SUMMARY_CONTENT" ] && echo "WORK UNIT SUMMARY:
213	---
214	$SUMMARY_CONTENT
215	---
216	")
217	
218	$([ "$MODE" = "update" ] && echo "EXISTING DOC CONTENT:
219	---
220	$EXISTING_DOC
221	---
222	")
223	
224	Produce the complete file content now.
225	EOF
226	)
227	
228	    NEW_CONTENT=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$PROMPT" 2>&1)
229	    EXIT_CODE=$?
230	
231	    if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
232	        echo "update-docs: failed to update $doc_path (exit $EXIT_CODE)" >&2
233	        # Write a proposal file so the update isn't lost
234	        PROPOSAL_PATH="${doc_path}.update-proposal-$(date +%H%M%S)"
235	        {
236	            echo "# Doc Update Failed"
237	            echo ""
238	            echo "Generated: $(date -Iseconds)"
239	            echo "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
240	            echo ""
241	            echo "Output:"
242	            echo "\`\`\`"
243	            echo "$NEW_CONTENT"
244	            echo "\`\`\`"
245	        } > "$PROPOSAL_PATH"
246	        continue
247	    fi
248	
249	    # Strip any wrapping markdown code fences claude might have added despite our instructions
250	    NEW_CONTENT=$(echo "$NEW_CONTENT" | sed -E '1s/^```(markdown|md)?$//' | sed -E '$s/^```$//')
251	
252	    # Sanity check: must start with frontmatter
253	    if ! echo "$NEW_CONTENT" | head -1 | grep -q '^---'; then
254	        echo "update-docs: invalid output for $doc_path (no frontmatter); writing as proposal" >&2
255	        PROPOSAL_PATH="${doc_path}.update-proposal-$(date +%H%M%S)"
256	        echo "$NEW_CONTENT" > "$PROPOSAL_PATH"
257	        continue
258	    fi
259	
260	    # Auto-apply per the user's preference
261	    echo "$NEW_CONTENT" > "$doc_path"
262	    echo "update-docs: wrote $doc_path ($MODE)"
263	done
264	
265	exit 0
266

#!/bin/bash
# update-docs.sh — Update interim documentation based on work unit changes
#
# Usage:
#   update-docs.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID           # auto-detect affected docs
#   update-docs.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID SLUG      # update specific doc by slug
#
# The auto-detect mode:
#   1. Identifies files modified in this work unit
#   2. Finds existing interim docs whose `implementation:` field references
#      any of those files
#   3. Updates those docs to reflect the new behavior
#   4. If no existing doc covers a modified file, creates a new interim doc
#
# The targeted mode (with SLUG):
#   1. Reads the existing doc at .claude/docs/interim/SLUG.md
#   2. Updates it based on recent work unit content

set -uo pipefail

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

LOG_PATH="$1"
SESSION_ID="$2"
TARGET_SLUG="${3:-}"

if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    echo "update-docs: log not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi

ensure_workflow_dirs

LOG_FILENAME=$(basename "$LOG_PATH" .md)
LOG_CONTENT=$(cat "$LOG_PATH")
TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)

# Try to find the corresponding summary
SUMMARY_PATH="${WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_summary.md"
SUMMARY_CONTENT=""
if [ -f "$SUMMARY_PATH" ]; then
    SUMMARY_CONTENT="$(cat "$SUMMARY_PATH")"
fi

# ============================================================================
# Identify modified files
# ============================================================================

FILES_MODIFIED=$(grep -oE '#(WRITE|EDIT|MULTIEDIT) `[^`]+`' "$LOG_PATH" | grep -oE '`[^`]+`' | tr -d '`' | sort -u)

if [ -z "$FILES_MODIFIED" ] && [ -z "$TARGET_SLUG" ]; then
    echo "update-docs: no files modified and no target slug; nothing to do"
    exit 0
fi

# ============================================================================
# Identify affected docs
# ============================================================================

AFFECTED_DOCS=()

if [ -n "$TARGET_SLUG" ]; then
    TARGET_DOC="${WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR}/${TARGET_SLUG}.md"
    if [ -f "$TARGET_DOC" ]; then
        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$TARGET_DOC")
    else
        # Doc doesn't exist; we'll create it below
        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$TARGET_DOC")
    fi
else
    # Auto-detect: scan existing interim docs for implementation: entries matching files
    if [ -d "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r f; do
            if grep -qF -- "$f" "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null; then
                while IFS= read -r doc; do
                    # Check if this doc actually references this file in its implementation: section
                    if grep -A 20 'implementation:' "$doc" 2>/dev/null | grep -qF -- "$f"; then
                        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$doc")
                    fi
                done < <(grep -lF -- "$f" "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null)
            fi
        done <<< "$FILES_MODIFIED"
    fi

    # Deduplicate
    if [ ${#AFFECTED_DOCS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
        readarray -t AFFECTED_DOCS < <(printf '%s\n' "${AFFECTED_DOCS[@]}" | sort -u)
    fi
fi

# ============================================================================
# Update each affected doc
# ============================================================================

if [ ${#AFFECTED_DOCS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    # No existing docs cover the modified files. Create a new interim doc.
    # We'll let claude -p figure out an appropriate slug from the work unit.

    NEW_SLUG_PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
Based on this work unit summary and log, suggest a short kebab-case slug
(3-6 words, lowercase, hyphen-separated) for an interim documentation file
covering the feature or area this work unit touched.

Output ONLY the slug, nothing else. No quotes, no explanation.

Summary:
$SUMMARY_CONTENT

Log:
$LOG_CONTENT
EOF
)

    NEW_SLUG=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$NEW_SLUG_PROMPT" 2>&1 | tr -d ' \n' | head -c 60)
    if [ -z "$NEW_SLUG" ] || ! echo "$NEW_SLUG" | grep -qE '^[a-z0-9-]+$'; then
        # Fallback to a generic slug based on the log filename
        NEW_SLUG=$(slugify "$LOG_FILENAME" | head -c 50)
    fi

    NEW_DOC="${WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR}/${NEW_SLUG}.md"
    AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$NEW_DOC")
fi

for doc_path in "${AFFECTED_DOCS[@]}"; do
    SLUG=$(basename "$doc_path" .md)

    if [ -f "$doc_path" ]; then
        EXISTING_DOC=$(cat "$doc_path")
        MODE="update"
    else
        EXISTING_DOC=""
        MODE="create"
    fi

    PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
You are maintaining an interim design document for a Claude Code project.

Mode: $MODE
Document slug: $SLUG
Document path: $doc_path

This is an INTERIM doc (status: interim) under .claude/docs/interim/. It will
eventually be merged into a canonical structure. For now, keep it focused on
describing CURRENT REALITY of the code.

Schema for interim docs (frontmatter at top of file, in YAML between --- lines):

  id: $SLUG
  title: <human-readable title>
  status: interim
  created: <YYYY-MM-DD, set once on creation>
  last_updated: <YYYY-MM-DD, today's date when updating>
  last_synced_with_code: <YYYY-MM-DD, today's date>
  work_units:
    - <list of work-unit log filenames that contributed>
  implementation:
    - <list of code file paths this doc describes>
  related:
    - <informal list of related doc slugs>

Body sections (in this order):
  # <Title>
  <1-3 sentence summary>

  ## Purpose
  <what this feature/area does>

  ## Behavior
  <how it behaves, including caveats>

  ## Implementation Notes
  <where the code lives, key design decisions>

  ## Changelog
  <!-- IGNORE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ASKED TO REVIEW DOCUMENT HISTORY -->
  ### YYYY-MM-DD
  <Description of changes made on this date. Most recent on top.>

YOUR JOB:
$(if [ "$MODE" = "create" ]; then
echo "- This doc does NOT yet exist. Create it fresh based on the work unit log and summary."
echo "- Set 'created' and 'last_updated' and 'last_synced_with_code' all to: $TODAY"
echo "- Populate work_units with: $LOG_FILENAME"
echo "- Populate implementation with the file paths actually modified in this work unit"
echo "- Add an initial changelog entry dated $TODAY describing the creation"
else
echo "- This doc EXISTS. Update it to reflect new behavior from this work unit."
echo "- Preserve the existing 'created' date (do NOT change it)"
echo "- Update 'last_updated' and 'last_synced_with_code' to: $TODAY"
echo "- APPEND the current work-unit log filename to work_units (if not already there): $LOG_FILENAME"
echo "- UPDATE implementation list if new files were touched"
echo "- UPDATE body sections to reflect new behavior (overwrite, don't append, in body sections)"
echo "- ADD a new changelog entry at the TOP of the Changelog section dated $TODAY, describing what changed"
echo "- KEEP previous changelog entries in place beneath the new one"
fi)

OUTPUT RULES:
- Output ONLY the complete file content (frontmatter + body), nothing else
- Start with the opening --- of frontmatter
- Do NOT include preamble, explanation, or markdown code fences around the output
- The output replaces the entire file contents

WORK UNIT LOG:
---
$LOG_CONTENT
---

$([ -n "$SUMMARY_CONTENT" ] && echo "WORK UNIT SUMMARY:
---
$SUMMARY_CONTENT
---
")

$([ "$MODE" = "update" ] && echo "EXISTING DOC CONTENT:
---
$EXISTING_DOC
---
")

Produce the complete file content now.
EOF
)

    NEW_CONTENT=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$PROMPT" 2>&1)
    EXIT_CODE=$?

    if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "update-docs: failed to update $doc_path (exit $EXIT_CODE)" >&2
        # Write a proposal file so the update isn't lost
        PROPOSAL_PATH="${doc_path}.update-proposal-$(date +%H%M%S)"
        {
            echo "# Doc Update Failed"
            echo ""
            echo "Generated: $(date -Iseconds)"
            echo "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
            echo ""
            echo "Output:"
            echo "\`\`\`"
            echo "$NEW_CONTENT"
            echo "\`\`\`"
        } > "$PROPOSAL_PATH"
        continue
    fi

    # Strip any wrapping markdown code fences claude might have added despite our instructions
    NEW_CONTENT=$(echo "$NEW_CONTENT" | sed -E '1s/^```(markdown|md)?$//' | sed -E '$s/^```$//')

    # Sanity check: must start with frontmatter
    if ! echo "$NEW_CONTENT" | head -1 | grep -q '^---'; then
        echo "update-docs: invalid output for $doc_path (no frontmatter); writing as proposal" >&2
        PROPOSAL_PATH="${doc_path}.update-proposal-$(date +%H%M%S)"
        echo "$NEW_CONTENT" > "$PROPOSAL_PATH"
        continue
    fi

    # Auto-apply per the user's preference
    echo "$NEW_CONTENT" > "$doc_path"
    echo "update-docs: wrote $doc_path ($MODE)"
done

exit 0

lastPrompt: Can you provide me the code for the review, deepreview, and wrapup hooks/functions ?

aiTitle: Audit UI architecture and create recommendations

content.text:
Here's the full picture:

---

## Skill definitions (slash command specs)

These live in `.claude/commands/` and are thin wrappers — they just call the shell scripts:

- **`/wrapup`** — runs all three scripts in sequence: `generate-summary.sh` → `run-review.sh shallow` → `update-docs.sh`
- **`/review`** — runs `run-review.sh shallow`
- **`/deepreview`** — runs `run-review.sh deep`
- **`/summarize`** — runs `generate-summary.sh` alone

All four read the current log path from `.claude/state/current_log.txt` and pass `$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID`.

---

## The three scripts

**`generate-summary.sh`** — calls `claude -p` with a structured prompt asking for a 6-section summary (purpose, decisions, what was built, deferred, open questions, files modified). Writes to `.claude/summaries/`.

**`run-review.sh`** — takes a `DEPTH` arg (`shallow` or `deep`). Extracts modified files from the log by scanning for `#WRITE`/`#EDIT`/`#MULTIEDIT` tags, runs `git diff HEAD` against those files, then calls `claude -p`. Shallow mode sends only the diff; deep mode also sends full file contents (truncated at 30KB/file). Writes to `.claude/reviews/`.

**`update-docs.sh`** — auto-detects which interim docs in `.claude/docs/interim/` reference the modified files (by scanning their `implementation:` frontmatter field), then calls `claude -p` to update or create each doc. If the output lacks frontmatter it falls back to a `.update-proposal-HHMMSS` sidecar file rather than corrupting the doc.

**`lib-common.sh`** — shared library sourced by all three scripts. Provides: directory paths, `claude_p_with_timeout()` (writes prompt to tmpfile to avoid ARG_MAX), log rotation logic (idle threshold: 4 hours), turn state recording, and tag classification (`[initprompt]`, `[phaseprompt]`, `[wrapup]`, etc.).

4	# Fires before Bash commands execute. Responsibilities:
5	#   1. Detect catastrophically dangerous commands (rm -rf root, dd to disk, etc.)
6	#   2. Block them with a clear reason
7	#
8	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
9	#   - tool_name: "Bash"
10	#   - tool_input.command: the command string
11	#
12	# Output:
13	#   - exit 0 with no JSON: command proceeds
14	#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": blocked
15	#
16	# Philosophy: this hook only blocks things that are almost certainly destructive
17	# mistakes. Normal cleanup commands (rm of build artifacts, etc.) are allowed.
18	# If you find yourself fighting this hook, expand the allowlist patterns below.
19	
20	set -uo pipefail
21	
22	HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-bash"
23	export HOOK_NAME
24	
25	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
26	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
27	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null

29	fi

32	COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""')
33	
34	if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
35	    exit 0
36	fi
37	
38	# ============================================================================
39	# DANGEROUS PATTERNS
40	# ============================================================================
41	# Each entry is "pattern|description". Patterns are bash-extended regex,
42	# matched case-insensitively against the command.
43	
44	DANGEROUS_PATTERNS=(
45	    # rm -rf on root or home
46	    'rm[[:space:]]+(-[a-zA-Z]*r[a-zA-Z]*f|-[a-zA-Z]*f[a-zA-Z]*r|-rf|-fr)[[:space:]]+(/$|/[[:space:]]|/\*|\$HOME|~/?$|/home/?$|/root/?$)|rm -rf on root or home directory'
47	
48	    # dd to a block device
49	    'dd[[:space:]].*of=/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|dd to a block device'
50	
51	    # mkfs on a block device
52	    'mkfs.*/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|mkfs on a block device'
53	
54	    # Fork bomb
55	    ':\(\)\{[[:space:]]*:\|:&[[:space:]]*\}|Fork bomb pattern'
56	
57	    # chmod 000 on critical paths
58	    'chmod[[:space:]]+(0?)?000[[:space:]]+(/$|/etc|/bin|/usr|/var)|chmod 000 on system directory'
59	
60	    # Pipe curl/wget to shell
61	    'curl[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping curl to shell'
62	    'wget[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping wget to shell'

64	    # Force-push to main/master
65	    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+.*--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'
66	    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'
67	
68	    # Disable firewall
69	    'ufw[[:space:]]+disable|Disabling firewall (ufw)'
70	    'iptables[[:space:]]+-F|Flushing iptables rules'
71	
72	    # Modify root authorized_keys
73	    '(echo|cat).*>>?[[:space:]]*/root/\.ssh/authorized_keys|Modifying root authorized_keys'
74	)
75	
76	for entry in "${DANGEROUS_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
77	    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
78	    desc="${entry##*|}"
79	    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
80	        REASON="Safety check blocked this command. Detected: $desc. Command was: $COMMAND. If this is intentional, run it manually outside of Claude Code, or modify .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-bash.sh to remove this pattern."
81	
82	        if command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
83	            hook_debug "BLOCKED: $desc — command: $COMMAND"
84	        fi

86	        jq -nc \

89	        exit 0
90	    fi
91	done
92	
93	# Command is fine; allow
94	exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# pre-tool-use-bash.sh — PreToolUse hook for Bash
#
# Fires before Bash commands execute. Responsibilities:
#   1. Detect catastrophically dangerous commands (rm -rf root, dd to disk, etc.)
#   2. Block them with a clear reason
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - tool_name: "Bash"
#   - tool_input.command: the command string
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 with no JSON: command proceeds
#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": blocked
#
# Philosophy: this hook only blocks things that are almost certainly destructive
# mistakes. Normal cleanup commands (rm of build artifacts, etc.) are allowed.
# If you find yourself fighting this hook, expand the allowlist patterns below.

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-bash"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

INPUT=$(cat)
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""')

if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# ============================================================================
# DANGEROUS PATTERNS
# ============================================================================
# Each entry is "pattern|description". Patterns are bash-extended regex,
# matched case-insensitively against the command.

DANGEROUS_PATTERNS=(
    # rm -rf on root or home
    'rm[[:space:]]+(-[a-zA-Z]*r[a-zA-Z]*f|-[a-zA-Z]*f[a-zA-Z]*r|-rf|-fr)[[:space:]]+(/$|/[[:space:]]|/\*|\$HOME|~/?$|/home/?$|/root/?$)|rm -rf on root or home directory'

    # dd to a block device
    'dd[[:space:]].*of=/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|dd to a block device'

    # mkfs on a block device
    'mkfs.*/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|mkfs on a block device'

    # Fork bomb
    ':\(\)\{[[:space:]]*:\|:&[[:space:]]*\}|Fork bomb pattern'

    # chmod 000 on critical paths
    'chmod[[:space:]]+(0?)?000[[:space:]]+(/$|/etc|/bin|/usr|/var)|chmod 000 on system directory'

    # Pipe curl/wget to shell
    'curl[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping curl to shell'
    'wget[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping wget to shell'

    # Force-push to main/master
    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+.*--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'
    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'

    # Disable firewall
    'ufw[[:space:]]+disable|Disabling firewall (ufw)'
    'iptables[[:space:]]+-F|Flushing iptables rules'

    # Modify root authorized_keys
    '(echo|cat).*>>?[[:space:]]*/root/\.ssh/authorized_keys|Modifying root authorized_keys'
)

for entry in "${DANGEROUS_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
    desc="${entry##*|}"
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
        REASON="Safety check blocked this command. Detected: $desc. Command was: $COMMAND. If this is intentional, run it manually outside of Claude Code, or modify .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-bash.sh to remove this pattern."

        if command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            hook_debug "BLOCKED: $desc — command: $COMMAND"
        fi

        jq -nc \
            --arg reason "$REASON" \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
        exit 0
    fi
done

# Command is fine; allow
exit 0

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# pre-tool-use-edit.sh — PreToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit
3	#
4	# Fires before file writes. Responsibilities:
5	#   1. Scan the content being written for common credential/secret patterns
6	#   2. Block the write if matches are found, with a clear reason
7	#
8	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
9	#   - tool_name, tool_input.{file_path, content, new_string, ...}
10	#
11	# Output:
12	#   - exit 0 with no JSON: write proceeds
13	#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": write blocked
14	#
15	# Note: This is a moderate-aggressiveness scanner. Patterns chosen for low
16	# false-positive rate. If you find this catching too many false positives,
17	# adjust SECRET_PATTERNS below. If you find it missing real secrets, ditto.
18	
19	set -uo pipefail
20	
21	HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-edit"
22	export HOOK_NAME
23	
24	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
25	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
26	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
27	    source "$LIB_PATH"
28	fi
29	
30	INPUT=$(cat)
31	
32	TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
33	FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')
34	
35	# Extract content based on which tool fired
36	CONTENT=""
37	case "$TOOL_NAME" in
38	    Write)
39	        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // ""')
40	        ;;
41	    Edit)
42	        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // ""')
43	        ;;
44	    MultiEdit)
45	        # MultiEdit has an edits array, each with new_string
46	        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '[.tool_input.edits[]?.new_string // ""] | join("\n")')
47	        ;;
48	    *)
49	        # Unknown tool variant; allow through
50	        exit 0
51	        ;;
52	esac
53	
54	# Skip empty content
55	if [ -z "$CONTENT" ]; then
56	    exit 0
57	fi
58	
59	# Skip if the file path looks like a test fixture, documentation, or
60	# explicitly-marked sample. Reduces false positives for example credentials
61	# in docs.
62	case "$FILE_PATH" in
63	    *test*|*tests*|*spec*|*fixture*|*example*|*sample*|*.md|*.rst|*.txt)
64	        # These get a lighter scan — only check for very specific real-key prefixes
65	        SCAN_MODE="light"
66	        ;;
67	    *)
68	        SCAN_MODE="full"
69	        ;;
70	esac
71	
72	# ============================================================================
73	# SECRET PATTERNS
74	# ============================================================================
75	# Each pattern below is a regex matched case-insensitively against content.
76	# Patterns are designed to catch real-world credentials with low false positives.
77	#
78	# To add a new pattern: append a "pattern|description" line to SECRET_PATTERNS.
79	# To remove a pattern: delete its line.
80	# To tune sensitivity: edit the regexes themselves (more specific = fewer FPs).
81	
82	SECRET_PATTERNS=(
83	    # AWS keys
84	    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
85	    'aws_secret_access_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}|AWS Secret Key'
86	
87	    # GitHub tokens
88	    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
89	    'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}|GitHub fine-grained PAT'
90	
91	    # OpenAI
92	    'sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20}T3BlbkFJ[A-Za-z0-9]{20}|OpenAI API key (legacy)'
93	    'sk-proj-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,}|OpenAI project key'
94	
95	    # Anthropic
96	    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'
97	
98	    # Stripe
99	    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
100	    'rk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live restricted key'
101	
102	    # Google
103	    'AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}|Google API key'
104	
105	    # Slack
106	    'xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}|Slack token'
107	
108	    # Generic high-entropy patterns near credential identifiers
109	    # Only used in "full" scan mode to reduce false positives in docs/tests
110	    'private_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
111	)
112	
113	# Light mode patterns: only obvious real keys, skip identifier-based heuristics
114	LIGHT_PATTERNS=(
115	    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
116	    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
117	    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'
118	    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
119	    '-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
120	)
121	
122	if [ "$SCAN_MODE" = "light" ]; then
123	    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${LIGHT_PATTERNS[@]}")
124	else
125	    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${SECRET_PATTERNS[@]}")
126	fi
127	
128	# Scan
129	MATCHED_PATTERN=""
130	MATCHED_DESC=""
131	for entry in "${PATTERNS_TO_USE[@]}"; do
132	    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
133	    desc="${entry##*|}"
134	    if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
135	        MATCHED_PATTERN="$pattern"
136	        MATCHED_DESC="$desc"
137	        break
138	    fi
139	done
140	
141	if [ -n "$MATCHED_PATTERN" ]; then
142	    # Block the write
143	    REASON="Secret scanner blocked this write. Detected pattern matching: $MATCHED_DESC. If this is a false positive (example credential, test fixture, etc.), move the file to a path containing 'test', 'example', 'fixture', or 'sample', or modify the secret scanner in .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-edit.sh to exclude this pattern."
144	
145	    if [ -n "${HOOK_NAME+x}" ] && command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
146	        hook_debug "BLOCKED: $FILE_PATH matched pattern: $MATCHED_DESC"
147	    fi
148	
149	    jq -nc \
150	        --arg reason "$REASON" \
151	        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
152	    exit 0
153	fi
154	
155	# No secrets detected; allow
156	exit 0
157

#!/bin/bash
# pre-tool-use-edit.sh — PreToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit
#
# Fires before file writes. Responsibilities:
#   1. Scan the content being written for common credential/secret patterns
#   2. Block the write if matches are found, with a clear reason
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - tool_name, tool_input.{file_path, content, new_string, ...}
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 with no JSON: write proceeds
#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": write blocked
#
# Note: This is a moderate-aggressiveness scanner. Patterns chosen for low
# false-positive rate. If you find this catching too many false positives,
# adjust SECRET_PATTERNS below. If you find it missing real secrets, ditto.

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-edit"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

INPUT=$(cat)

TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

# Extract content based on which tool fired
CONTENT=""
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Write)
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // ""')
        ;;
    Edit)
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // ""')
        ;;
    MultiEdit)
        # MultiEdit has an edits array, each with new_string
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '[.tool_input.edits[]?.new_string // ""] | join("\n")')
        ;;
    *)
        # Unknown tool variant; allow through
        exit 0
        ;;
esac

# Skip empty content
if [ -z "$CONTENT" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# Skip if the file path looks like a test fixture, documentation, or
# explicitly-marked sample. Reduces false positives for example credentials
# in docs.
case "$FILE_PATH" in
    *test*|*tests*|*spec*|*fixture*|*example*|*sample*|*.md|*.rst|*.txt)
        # These get a lighter scan — only check for very specific real-key prefixes
        SCAN_MODE="light"
        ;;
    *)
        SCAN_MODE="full"
        ;;
esac

# ============================================================================
# SECRET PATTERNS
# ============================================================================
# Each pattern below is a regex matched case-insensitively against content.
# Patterns are designed to catch real-world credentials with low false positives.
#
# To add a new pattern: append a "pattern|description" line to SECRET_PATTERNS.
# To remove a pattern: delete its line.
# To tune sensitivity: edit the regexes themselves (more specific = fewer FPs).

SECRET_PATTERNS=(
    # AWS keys
    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
    'aws_secret_access_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}|AWS Secret Key'

    # GitHub tokens
    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
    'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}|GitHub fine-grained PAT'

    # OpenAI
    'sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20}T3BlbkFJ[A-Za-z0-9]{20}|OpenAI API key (legacy)'
    'sk-proj-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,}|OpenAI project key'

    # Anthropic
    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'

    # Stripe
    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
    'rk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live restricted key'

    # Google
    'AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}|Google API key'

    # Slack
    'xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}|Slack token'

    # Generic high-entropy patterns near credential identifiers
    # Only used in "full" scan mode to reduce false positives in docs/tests
    'private_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
)

# Light mode patterns: only obvious real keys, skip identifier-based heuristics
LIGHT_PATTERNS=(
    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'
    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
    '-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
)

if [ "$SCAN_MODE" = "light" ]; then
    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${LIGHT_PATTERNS[@]}")
else
    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${SECRET_PATTERNS[@]}")
fi

# Scan
MATCHED_PATTERN=""
MATCHED_DESC=""
for entry in "${PATTERNS_TO_USE[@]}"; do
    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
    desc="${entry##*|}"
    if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
        MATCHED_PATTERN="$pattern"
        MATCHED_DESC="$desc"
        break
    fi
done

if [ -n "$MATCHED_PATTERN" ]; then
    # Block the write
    REASON="Secret scanner blocked this write. Detected pattern matching: $MATCHED_DESC. If this is a false positive (example credential, test fixture, etc.), move the file to a path containing 'test', 'example', 'fixture', or 'sample', or modify the secret scanner in .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-edit.sh to exclude this pattern."

    if [ -n "${HOOK_NAME+x}" ] && command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        hook_debug "BLOCKED: $FILE_PATH matched pattern: $MATCHED_DESC"
    fi

    jq -nc \
        --arg reason "$REASON" \
        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
    exit 0
fi

# No secrets detected; allow
exit 0

2	# post-tool-use.sh — PostToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit
3	#
4	# Fires after file writes succeed. Responsibilities:
5	#   1. Append a structural-tagged entry to the current work-unit log
6	#      indicating which file was modified.
7	#
8	# This is the cheap, mechanical part of the verification system. The expensive
9	# LLM-based summary/review happens in the Stop hook, and only on [wrapup].
10	#
11	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
12	#   - tool_name, tool_input.{file_path, ...}, tool_response
13	#
14	# Output:
15	#   - exit 0 always; no decision control needed for PostToolUse
16	
17	set -uo pipefail
18	
19	HOOK_NAME="post-tool-use"
20	export HOOK_NAME
21	
22	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
23	if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
24	    exit 0  # Fail silently rather than disrupt the user's session
25	fi
26	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
27	source "$LIB_PATH"
28	
29	INPUT=$(cat)
30	
31	TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
32	FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')
33	SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
34	
35	export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
36	ensure_workflow_dirs
37	
38	# Determine the current work-unit log
39	LOG_PATH=$(get_current_log_path)
40	
41	# Determine the kind of change
42	TAG="#FILE"
43	case "$TOOL_NAME" in
44	    Write) TAG="#WRITE" ;;
45	    Edit) TAG="#EDIT" ;;
46	    MultiEdit) TAG="#MULTIEDIT" ;;
47	esac
48	
49	# For Edit/MultiEdit, we can also note approximate change size by looking at
50	# the old_string/new_string lengths.
51	SIZE_HINT=""
52	case "$TOOL_NAME" in
53	    Edit)
54	        OLD_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.old_string // "" | length')
55	        NEW_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // "" | length')
56	        SIZE_HINT=" (${OLD_LEN}→${NEW_LEN} chars)"
57	        ;;
58	    Write)
59	        NEW_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // "" | length')
60	        SIZE_HINT=" (${NEW_LEN} chars written)"
61	        ;;
62	    MultiEdit)
63	        EDIT_COUNT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.edits // [] | length')
64	        SIZE_HINT=" (${EDIT_COUNT} edits)"
65	        ;;
66	esac
67	
68	# Append to log
69	{
70	    echo ""
71	    echo "## $TAG \`$FILE_PATH\`$SIZE_HINT"
72	    echo ""
73	    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
74	    echo ""
75	} >> "$LOG_PATH"
76	
77	hook_debug "Logged $TAG for $FILE_PATH"
78	
79	exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# post-tool-use.sh — PostToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit
#
# Fires after file writes succeed. Responsibilities:
#   1. Append a structural-tagged entry to the current work-unit log
#      indicating which file was modified.
#
# This is the cheap, mechanical part of the verification system. The expensive
# LLM-based summary/review happens in the Stop hook, and only on [wrapup].
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - tool_name, tool_input.{file_path, ...}, tool_response
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 always; no decision control needed for PostToolUse

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="post-tool-use"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    exit 0  # Fail silently rather than disrupt the user's session
fi
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "$LIB_PATH"

INPUT=$(cat)

TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')
SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')

export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
ensure_workflow_dirs

# Determine the current work-unit log
LOG_PATH=$(get_current_log_path)

# Determine the kind of change
TAG="#FILE"
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Write) TAG="#WRITE" ;;
    Edit) TAG="#EDIT" ;;
    MultiEdit) TAG="#MULTIEDIT" ;;
esac

# For Edit/MultiEdit, we can also note approximate change size by looking at
# the old_string/new_string lengths.
SIZE_HINT=""
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Edit)
        OLD_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.old_string // "" | length')
        NEW_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // "" | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${OLD_LEN}→${NEW_LEN} chars)"
        ;;
    Write)
        NEW_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // "" | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${NEW_LEN} chars written)"
        ;;
    MultiEdit)
        EDIT_COUNT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.edits // [] | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${EDIT_COUNT} edits)"
        ;;
esac

# Append to log
{
    echo ""
    echo "## $TAG \`$FILE_PATH\`$SIZE_HINT"
    echo ""
    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    echo ""
} >> "$LOG_PATH"

hook_debug "Logged $TAG for $FILE_PATH"

exit 0

2	# session-end.sh — SessionEnd hook
3	#
4	# Fires when a Claude Code session terminates. Responsibilities:
5	#   1. Archive the full transcript JSONL to .claude/archive/
6	#      (Claude Code's own transcripts get cleaned up after 30 days by default;
7	#       archiving preserves them indefinitely)
8	#
9	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
10	#   - session_id, transcript_path, reason
11	#
12	# Output:
13	#   - exit 0 always; SessionEnd cannot block
14	
15	set -uo pipefail
16	
17	HOOK_NAME="session-end"
18	export HOOK_NAME
19	
20	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
21	if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
22	    exit 0
23	fi
24	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
25	source "$LIB_PATH"
26	
27	INPUT=$(cat)
28	
29	SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
30	TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
31	REASON=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.reason // "unknown"')
32	
33	ensure_workflow_dirs
34	
35	if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ] || [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
36	    hook_debug "No transcript to archive (path=$TRANSCRIPT_PATH)"
37	    exit 0
38	fi
39	
40	# Archive with a name that includes session ID and timestamp
41	TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
42	SHORT_SID=$(short_session_id "$SESSION_ID")
43	ARCHIVE_PATH="${WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR}/session_${TIMESTAMP}_${SHORT_SID}_${REASON}.jsonl"
44	
45	if cp "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" "$ARCHIVE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then
46	    hook_debug "Archived transcript to $ARCHIVE_PATH"
47	else
48	    hook_debug "Failed to archive transcript from $TRANSCRIPT_PATH to $ARCHIVE_PATH"
49	fi
50	
51	# Optionally clean up old turn state files for this session (none should remain
52	# if Stop hook ran, but defensive)
53	rm -f "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${SESSION_ID}.json"
54	
55	exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# session-end.sh — SessionEnd hook
#
# Fires when a Claude Code session terminates. Responsibilities:
#   1. Archive the full transcript JSONL to .claude/archive/
#      (Claude Code's own transcripts get cleaned up after 30 days by default;
#       archiving preserves them indefinitely)
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - session_id, transcript_path, reason
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 always; SessionEnd cannot block

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="session-end"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    exit 0
fi
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "$LIB_PATH"

INPUT=$(cat)

SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
REASON=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.reason // "unknown"')

ensure_workflow_dirs

if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ] || [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    hook_debug "No transcript to archive (path=$TRANSCRIPT_PATH)"
    exit 0
fi

# Archive with a name that includes session ID and timestamp
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
SHORT_SID=$(short_session_id "$SESSION_ID")
ARCHIVE_PATH="${WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR}/session_${TIMESTAMP}_${SHORT_SID}_${REASON}.jsonl"

if cp "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" "$ARCHIVE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then
    hook_debug "Archived transcript to $ARCHIVE_PATH"
else
    hook_debug "Failed to archive transcript from $TRANSCRIPT_PATH to $ARCHIVE_PATH"
fi

# Optionally clean up old turn state files for this session (none should remain
# if Stop hook ran, but defensive)
rm -f "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${SESSION_ID}.json"

exit 0

content.text: Also the `extract-turn.sh` script that the log system uses:

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# extract-turn.sh — Pull a single turn out of the transcript JSONL into the
3	# work-unit log with structural tags.
4	#
5	# Usage: extract-turn.sh TRANSCRIPT_PATH START_OFFSET LOG_PATH PROMPT_TMPFILE
6	#
7	# Reads entries from TRANSCRIPT_PATH starting at byte offset START_OFFSET,
8	# parses each JSONL line, filters and tags them, and appends to LOG_PATH.
9	#
10	# This is the "extraction" we discussed — converts noisy transcript into
11	# clean tagged log entries. Structural tags only (no LLM analysis).
12	
13	set -uo pipefail
14	
15	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
16	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
17	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
18	    source "$LIB_PATH"
19	fi
20	
21	TRANSCRIPT_PATH="$1"
22	START_OFFSET="$2"
23	LOG_PATH="$3"
24	ORIG_PROMPT=$(cat "$4")         # $4 is a tempfile path written by stop.sh to avoid ARG_MAX
25	LAST_ASSISTANT_MSG=$(cat "$5" 2>/dev/null || echo "")  # $5 is last_assistant_message from Stop hook input (v2.1.143+)
26	
27	# Sanitize workflow tags so they don't re-trigger hooks when log content is
28	# embedded verbatim in claude -p review/summary prompts. Converts [tag] to
29	# ~tag~ — still readable in the log, but won't match tag detection patterns.
30	sanitize_tags() {
31	    sed -E 's/\[(initprompt|phaseprompt|wrapup|review|deepreview|docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+)\]/~\1~/g'
32	}
33	
34	if [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
35	    echo "extract-turn: transcript not found: $TRANSCRIPT_PATH" >&2
36	    exit 1
37	fi
38	
39	if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
40	    echo "extract-turn: log file not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
41	    exit 1
42	fi
43	
44	# Read the transcript content starting at START_OFFSET
45	# Use tail -c to skip to the byte offset
46	TRANSCRIPT_SLICE=$(tail -c +"$((START_OFFSET + 1))" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" 2>/dev/null)
47	
48	if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" ]; then
49	    # Nothing new in transcript. Two possible reasons:
50	    # 1. Timing: Stop hook fired before the assistant entry was flushed to JSONL.
51	    # 2. The turn had no new content (e.g. sub-session with an empty response).
52	    # Use last_assistant_message from the hook input (v2.1.143+) as a fallback
53	    # for case 1. If that's also empty, record a stub so the log stays parseable.
54	    {
55	        echo ""
56	        echo "## #PROMPT"
57	        echo ""
58	        echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
59	        echo ""
60	        echo "\`\`\`"
61	        echo "$ORIG_PROMPT" | sanitize_tags
62	        echo "\`\`\`"
63	        echo ""
64	        if [ -n "$LAST_ASSISTANT_MSG" ]; then
65	            echo "## #ASSISTANT"
66	            echo ""
67	            echo "$LAST_ASSISTANT_MSG" | sanitize_tags
68	            echo ""
69	        else
70	            echo "_(No new transcript entries since prompt submission)_"
71	            echo ""
72	        fi
73	        echo "## #TURN_END"
74	        echo ""
75	        echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
76	        echo ""
77	        echo "---"
78	        echo ""
79	    } >> "$LOG_PATH"
80	    exit 0
81	fi
82	
83	# ============================================================================
84	# Parse JSONL entries and append structural-tagged entries to the log
85	# ============================================================================
86	
87	# Append the prompt header
88	{
89	    echo ""
90	    echo "## #PROMPT"
91	    echo ""
92	    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
93	    echo ""
94	    echo "\`\`\`"
95	    echo "$ORIG_PROMPT" | sanitize_tags
96	    echo "\`\`\`"
97	    echo ""
98	} >> "$LOG_PATH"
99	
100	# Process each line of the transcript slice. Each line is a JSON object.
101	# We're looking for specific entry types and extracting useful content.
102	#
103	# The transcript JSONL format from Claude Code contains entries like:
104	#   { "type": "user", "message": {...} }
105	#   { "type": "assistant", "message": { "content": [...] } }
106	#   where content items have types: text, tool_use, tool_result, thinking
107	#
108	# We extract:
109	#   - assistant text → #ASSISTANT
110	#   - tool_use (Bash) → #BASH
111	#   - tool_use (Edit/Write/MultiEdit) → already covered by PostToolUse hook
112	#   - tool_use (Grep) → #GREP
113	#   - tool_use (Glob) → #GLOB
114	#   - tool_use (Read) → skipped (too noisy)
115	#   - tool_use (TodoWrite) → #TODO
116	#   - tool_use (Task) → #TASK
117	#   - tool_use (WebSearch/WebFetch) → #WEB
118	#   - tool_result with is_error=true → #ERROR
119	#
120	# We deliberately skip routine tool calls that don't add information.
121	
122	echo "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" | while IFS= read -r line; do
123	    # Skip empty lines
124	    if [ -z "$line" ]; then continue; fi
125	
126	    # Skip lines that aren't valid JSON
127	    if ! echo "$line" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then continue; fi
128	
129	    ENTRY_TYPE=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // ""')
130	
131	    case "$ENTRY_TYPE" in
132	        assistant)
133	            # Extract content items from the assistant message
134	            # Iterate through .message.content array
135	            CONTENT_ITEMS=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content // [] | .[]' 2>/dev/null)
136	
137	            echo "$CONTENT_ITEMS" | while IFS= read -r item; do
138	                if [ -z "$item" ]; then continue; fi
139	                ITEM_TYPE=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.type // ""')
140	
141	                case "$ITEM_TYPE" in
142	                    text)
143	                        TEXT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.text // ""')
144	                        if [ -n "$TEXT" ]; then
145	                            {
146	                                echo ""
147	                                echo "## #ASSISTANT"
148	                                echo ""
149	                                echo "$TEXT" | sanitize_tags
150	                                echo ""
151	                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
152	                        fi
153	                        ;;
154	                    tool_use)
155	                        TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.name // ""')
156	                        case "$TOOL_NAME" in
157	                            Bash)
158	                                CMD=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.command // ""')
159	                                DESC=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.description // ""')
160	                                {
161	                                    echo ""
162	                                    echo "## #BASH"
163	                                    [ -n "$DESC" ] && echo "_${DESC}_"
164	                                    echo ""
165	                                    echo "\`\`\`bash"
166	                                    echo "$CMD"
167	                                    echo "\`\`\`"
168	                                    echo ""
169	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
170	                                ;;
171	                            Grep)
172	                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
173	                                PATH_ARG=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.path // ""')
174	                                {
175	                                    echo ""
176	                                    echo "## #GREP \`$PATTERN\` in \`$PATH_ARG\`"
177	                                    echo ""
178	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
179	                                ;;
180	                            Glob)
181	                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
182	                                {
183	                                    echo ""
184	                                    echo "## #GLOB \`$PATTERN\`"
185	                                    echo ""
186	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
187	                                ;;
188	                            TodoWrite)
189	                                {
190	                                    echo ""
191	                                    echo "## #TODO Todo list updated"
192	                                    echo ""
193	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
194	                                ;;
195	                            Task|Agent)
196	                                SUBAGENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.subagent_type // .input.description // ""')
197	                                {
198	                                    echo ""
199	                                    echo "## #TASK Subagent: $SUBAGENT"
200	                                    echo ""
201	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
202	                                ;;
203	                            WebSearch|WebFetch)
204	                                QUERY=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.query // .input.url // ""')
205	                                {
206	                                    echo ""
207	                                    echo "## #WEB $TOOL_NAME: $QUERY"
208	                                    echo ""
209	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
210	                                ;;

#!/bin/bash
# extract-turn.sh — Pull a single turn out of the transcript JSONL into the
# work-unit log with structural tags.
#
# Usage: extract-turn.sh TRANSCRIPT_PATH START_OFFSET LOG_PATH PROMPT_TMPFILE
#
# Reads entries from TRANSCRIPT_PATH starting at byte offset START_OFFSET,
# parses each JSONL line, filters and tags them, and appends to LOG_PATH.
#
# This is the "extraction" we discussed — converts noisy transcript into
# clean tagged log entries. Structural tags only (no LLM analysis).

set -uo pipefail

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

TRANSCRIPT_PATH="$1"
START_OFFSET="$2"
LOG_PATH="$3"
ORIG_PROMPT=$(cat "$4")         # $4 is a tempfile path written by stop.sh to avoid ARG_MAX
LAST_ASSISTANT_MSG=$(cat "$5" 2>/dev/null || echo "")  # $5 is last_assistant_message from Stop hook input (v2.1.143+)

# Sanitize workflow tags so they don't re-trigger hooks when log content is
# embedded verbatim in claude -p review/summary prompts. Converts [tag] to
# ~tag~ — still readable in the log, but won't match tag detection patterns.
sanitize_tags() {
    sed -E 's/\[(initprompt|phaseprompt|wrapup|review|deepreview|docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+)\]/~\1~/g'
}

if [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    echo "extract-turn: transcript not found: $TRANSCRIPT_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    echo "extract-turn: log file not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Read the transcript content starting at START_OFFSET
# Use tail -c to skip to the byte offset
TRANSCRIPT_SLICE=$(tail -c +"$((START_OFFSET + 1))" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" 2>/dev/null)

if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" ]; then
    # Nothing new in transcript. Two possible reasons:
    # 1. Timing: Stop hook fired before the assistant entry was flushed to JSONL.
    # 2. The turn had no new content (e.g. sub-session with an empty response).
    # Use last_assistant_message from the hook input (v2.1.143+) as a fallback
    # for case 1. If that's also empty, record a stub so the log stays parseable.
    {
        echo ""
        echo "## #PROMPT"
        echo ""
        echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
        echo ""
        echo "\`\`\`"
        echo "$ORIG_PROMPT" | sanitize_tags
        echo "\`\`\`"
        echo ""
        if [ -n "$LAST_ASSISTANT_MSG" ]; then
            echo "## #ASSISTANT"
            echo ""
            echo "$LAST_ASSISTANT_MSG" | sanitize_tags
            echo ""
        else
            echo "_(No new transcript entries since prompt submission)_"
            echo ""
        fi
        echo "## #TURN_END"
        echo ""
        echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
        echo ""
        echo "---"
        echo ""
    } >> "$LOG_PATH"
    exit 0
fi

# ============================================================================
# Parse JSONL entries and append structural-tagged entries to the log
# ============================================================================

# Append the prompt header
{
    echo ""
    echo "## #PROMPT"
    echo ""
    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    echo ""
    echo "\`\`\`"
    echo "$ORIG_PROMPT" | sanitize_tags
    echo "\`\`\`"
    echo ""
} >> "$LOG_PATH"

# Process each line of the transcript slice. Each line is a JSON object.
# We're looking for specific entry types and extracting useful content.
#
# The transcript JSONL format from Claude Code contains entries like:
#   { "type": "user", "message": {...} }
#   { "type": "assistant", "message": { "content": [...] } }
#   where content items have types: text, tool_use, tool_result, thinking
#
# We extract:
#   - assistant text → #ASSISTANT
#   - tool_use (Bash) → #BASH
#   - tool_use (Edit/Write/MultiEdit) → already covered by PostToolUse hook
#   - tool_use (Grep) → #GREP
#   - tool_use (Glob) → #GLOB
#   - tool_use (Read) → skipped (too noisy)
#   - tool_use (TodoWrite) → #TODO
#   - tool_use (Task) → #TASK
#   - tool_use (WebSearch/WebFetch) → #WEB
#   - tool_result with is_error=true → #ERROR
#
# We deliberately skip routine tool calls that don't add information.

echo "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" | while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Skip empty lines
    if [ -z "$line" ]; then continue; fi

    # Skip lines that aren't valid JSON
    if ! echo "$line" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then continue; fi

    ENTRY_TYPE=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // ""')

    case "$ENTRY_TYPE" in
        assistant)
            # Extract content items from the assistant message
            # Iterate through .message.content array
            CONTENT_ITEMS=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content // [] | .[]' 2>/dev/null)

            echo "$CONTENT_ITEMS" | while IFS= read -r item; do
                if [ -z "$item" ]; then continue; fi
                ITEM_TYPE=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.type // ""')

                case "$ITEM_TYPE" in
                    text)
                        TEXT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.text // ""')
                        if [ -n "$TEXT" ]; then
                            {
                                echo ""
                                echo "## #ASSISTANT"
                                echo ""
                                echo "$TEXT" | sanitize_tags
                                echo ""
                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                        fi
                        ;;
                    tool_use)
                        TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.name // ""')
                        case "$TOOL_NAME" in
                            Bash)
                                CMD=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.command // ""')
                                DESC=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.description // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #BASH"
                                    [ -n "$DESC" ] && echo "_${DESC}_"
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "\`\`\`bash"
                                    echo "$CMD"
                                    echo "\`\`\`"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            Grep)
                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
                                PATH_ARG=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.path // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #GREP \`$PATTERN\` in \`$PATH_ARG\`"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            Glob)
                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #GLOB \`$PATTERN\`"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            TodoWrite)
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #TODO Todo list updated"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            Task|Agent)
                                SUBAGENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.subagent_type // .input.description // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #TASK Subagent: $SUBAGENT"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            WebSearch|WebFetch)
                                QUERY=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.query // .input.url // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #WEB $TOOL_NAME: $QUERY"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            # Edit, Write, MultiEdit are logged by post-tool-use.sh
                            # Read is intentionally skipped as too noisy
                            *) ;;
                        esac
                        ;;
                    tool_result)
                        IS_ERROR=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
                        if [ "$IS_ERROR" = "true" ]; then
                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.content // "" | if type == "string" then . else (tostring) end')
                            # Truncate very long errors
                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$ERR_CONTENT" | head -c 2000)
                            {
                                echo ""
                                echo "## #ERROR"
                                echo ""
                                echo "\`\`\`"
                                echo "$ERR_CONTENT"
                                echo "\`\`\`"
                                echo ""
                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                        fi
                        ;;
                    thinking)
                        # Skip thinking blocks — too verbose, not load-bearing for review
                        ;;
                esac
            done
            ;;
        user)
            # The user message is already captured at the top (#PROMPT).
            # Skip subsequent user messages (these are typically tool results being
            # threaded back to the model, not new user input).
            ;;
        system)
            # Skip system messages
            ;;
    esac
done

# Append a turn-end marker
{
    echo ""
    echo "## #TURN_END"
    echo ""
    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    echo ""
    echo "---"
    echo ""
} >> "$LOG_PATH"

exit 0

content.text:
That's the complete set. Here's a summary of what each file does:

---

**Hook entry points** (registered in `settings.json`, called by the harness):

| File | Trigger | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `pre-tool-use-bash.sh` | Before every `Bash` tool call | Blocks ~10 dangerous command patterns (rm -rf root, dd to device, force-push to main, etc.) |
| `pre-tool-use-edit.sh` | Before every `Write`/`Edit`/`MultiEdit` | Scans content for credential patterns (AWS keys, GitHub tokens, Anthropic keys, etc.); full scan for code files, light scan for docs/tests |
| `post-tool-use.sh` | After every `Write`/`Edit`/`MultiEdit` | Appends a `#WRITE`/`#EDIT`/`#MULTIEDIT` tag with file path and change size to the current work-unit log file |
| `session-end.sh` | When the session terminates | Archives the transcript JSONL to `.claude/archive/` for indefinite retention |

**Scripts** (called by the slash commands or by a Stop hook, not shown here):

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `extract-turn.sh` | Parses the transcript JSONL from a byte offset, emits `#PROMPT`/`#ASSISTANT`/`#BASH`/`#GREP`/`#ERROR`/etc. tags into the work-unit log; sanitizes `[initprompt]`/`[wrapup]` tags to `~tag~` so they don't re-trigger hooks when the log is fed back to `claude -p` |
| `generate-summary.sh` | Feeds the work-unit log to `claude -p` with a structured summary prompt; writes to `.claude/summaries/` |
| `run-review.sh` | Extracts modified files from the log, diffs them with `git diff HEAD`, feeds log + diff (+ full file contents in deep mode) to `claude -p`; writes to `.claude/reviews/` |
| `update-docs.sh` | Finds which interim docs reference the modified files, calls `claude -p` to update or create each doc; writes to `.claude/docs/interim/` |
| `lib-common.sh` | Shared library: directory paths, `claude_p_with_timeout()`, log rotation, turn state, tag classification, `slugify()` |

Here are the raw file contents:

**`pre-tool-use-bash.sh`**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# pre-tool-use-bash.sh — PreToolUse hook for Bash
#
# Fires before Bash commands execute. Responsibilities:
#   1. Detect catastrophically dangerous commands (rm -rf root, dd to disk, etc.)
#   2. Block them with a clear reason
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - tool_name: "Bash"
#   - tool_input.command: the command string
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 with no JSON: command proceeds
#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": blocked
#
# Philosophy: this hook only blocks things that are almost certainly destructive
# mistakes. Normal cleanup commands (rm of build artifacts, etc.) are allowed.
# If you find yourself fighting this hook, expand the allowlist patterns below.

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-bash"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

INPUT=$(cat)
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""')

if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# ============================================================================
# DANGEROUS PATTERNS
# ============================================================================
# Each entry is "pattern|description". Patterns are bash-extended regex,
# matched case-insensitively against the command.

DANGEROUS_PATTERNS=(
    # rm -rf on root or home
    'rm[[:space:]]+(-[a-zA-Z]*r[a-zA-Z]*f|-[a-zA-Z]*f[a-zA-Z]*r|-rf|-fr)[[:space:]]+(/$|/[[:space:]]|/\*|\$HOME|~/?$|/home/?$|/root/?$)|rm -rf on root or home directory'

    # dd to a block device
    'dd[[:space:]].*of=/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|dd to a block device'

    # mkfs on a block device
    'mkfs.*/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|mkfs on a block device'

    # Fork bomb
    ':\(\)\{[[:space:]]*:\|:&[[:space:]]*\}|Fork bomb pattern'

    # chmod 000 on critical paths
    'chmod[[:space:]]+(0?)?000[[:space:]]+(/$|/etc|/bin|/usr|/var)|chmod 000 on system directory'

    # Pipe curl/wget to shell
    'curl[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping curl to shell'
    'wget[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping wget to shell'

    # Force-push to main/master
    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+.*--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'
    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'

    # Disable firewall
    'ufw[[:space:]]+disable|Disabling firewall (ufw)'
    'iptables[[:space:]]+-F|Flushing iptables rules'

    # Modify root authorized_keys
    '(echo|cat).*>>?[[:space:]]*/root/\.ssh/authorized_keys|Modifying root authorized_keys'
)

for entry in "${DANGEROUS_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
    desc="${entry##*|}"
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
        REASON="Safety check blocked this command. Detected: $desc. Command was: $COMMAND. If this is intentional, run it manually outside of Claude Code, or modify .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-bash.sh to remove this pattern."

        if command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            hook_debug "BLOCKED: $desc — command: $COMMAND"
        fi

        jq -nc \
            --arg reason "$REASON" \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
        exit 0
    fi
done

# Command is fine; allow
exit 0
```

---

**`pre-tool-use-edit.sh`**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# pre-tool-use-edit.sh — PreToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit
#
# Fires before file writes. Responsibilities:
#   1. Scan the content being written for common credential/secret patterns
#   2. Block the write if matches are found, with a clear reason
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - tool_name, tool_input.{file_path, content, new_string, ...}
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 with no JSON: write proceeds
#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": write blocked
#
# Note: This is a moderate-aggressiveness scanner. Patterns chosen for low
# false-positive rate. If you find this catching too many false positives,
# adjust SECRET_PATTERNS below. If you find it missing real secrets, ditto.

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-edit"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

INPUT=$(cat)

TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

# Extract content based on which tool fired
CONTENT=""
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Write)
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // ""')
        ;;
    Edit)
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // ""')
        ;;
    MultiEdit)
        # MultiEdit has an edits array, each with new_string
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '[.tool_input.edits[]?.new_string // ""] | join("\n")')
        ;;
    *)
        exit 0
        ;;
esac

if [ -z "$CONTENT" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

case "$FILE_PATH" in
    *test*|*tests*|*spec*|*fixture*|*example*|*sample*|*.md|*.rst|*.txt)
        SCAN_MODE="light"
        ;;
    *)
        SCAN_MODE="full"
        ;;
esac

# ============================================================================
# SECRET PATTERNS
# ============================================================================

SECRET_PATTERNS=(
    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
    'aws_secret_access_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}|AWS Secret Key'
    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
    'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}|GitHub fine-grained PAT'
    'sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20}T3BlbkFJ[A-Za-z0-9]{20}|OpenAI API key (legacy)'
    'sk-proj-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,}|OpenAI project key'
    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'
    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
    'rk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live restricted key'
    'AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}|Google API key'
    'xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}|Slack token'
    'private_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
)

LIGHT_PATTERNS=(
    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'
    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
    '-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
)

if [ "$SCAN_MODE" = "light" ]; then
    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${LIGHT_PATTERNS[@]}")
else
    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${SECRET_PATTERNS[@]}")
fi

MATCHED_PATTERN=""
MATCHED_DESC=""
for entry in "${PATTERNS_TO_USE[@]}"; do
    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
    desc="${entry##*|}"
    if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
        MATCHED_PATTERN="$pattern"
        MATCHED_DESC="$desc"
        break
    fi
done

if [ -n "$MATCHED_PATTERN" ]; then
    REASON="Secret scanner blocked this write. Detected pattern matching: $MATCHED_DESC. If this is a false positive (example credential, test fixture, etc.), move the file to a path containing 'test', 'example', 'fixture', or 'sample', or modify the secret scanner in .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-edit.sh to exclude this pattern."

    if [ -n "${HOOK_NAME+x}" ] && command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        hook_debug "BLOCKED: $FILE_PATH matched pattern: $MATCHED_DESC"
    fi

    jq -nc \
        --arg reason "$REASON" \
        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
    exit 0
fi

exit 0
```

**`post-tool-use.sh`**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# post-tool-use.sh — PostToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="post-tool-use"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    exit 0
fi
source "$LIB_PATH"

INPUT=$(cat)

TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')
SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')

export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
ensure_workflow_dirs

LOG_PATH=$(get_current_log_path)

TAG="#FILE"
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Write) TAG="#WRITE" ;;
    Edit) TAG="#EDIT" ;;
    MultiEdit) TAG="#MULTIEDIT" ;;
esac

SIZE_HINT=""
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Edit)
        OLD_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.old_string // "" | length')
        NEW_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // "" | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${OLD_LEN}→${NEW_LEN} chars)"
        ;;
    Write)
        NEW_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // "" | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${NEW_LEN} chars written)"
        ;;
    MultiEdit)
        EDIT_COUNT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.edits // [] | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${EDIT_COUNT} edits)"
        ;;
esac

{
    echo ""
    echo "## $TAG \`$FILE_PATH\`$SIZE_HINT"
    echo ""
    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    echo ""
} >> "$LOG_PATH"

hook_debug "Logged $TAG for $FILE_PATH"

exit 0
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# session-end.sh — SessionEnd hook

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="session-end"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    exit 0
fi
source "$LIB_PATH"

INPUT=$(cat)

SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
REASON=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.reason // "unknown"')

ensure_workflow_dirs

if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ] || [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    hook_debug "No transcript to archive (path=$TRANSCRIPT_PATH)"
    exit 0
fi

TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
SHORT_SID=$(short_session_id "$SESSION_ID")
ARCHIVE_PATH="${WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR}/session_${TIMESTAMP}_${SHORT_SID}_${REASON}.jsonl"

if cp "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" "$ARCHIVE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then
    hook_debug "Archived transcript to $ARCHIVE_PATH"
else
    hook_debug "Failed to archive transcript from $TRANSCRIPT_PATH to $ARCHIVE_PATH"
fi

rm -f "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${SESSION_ID}.json"

exit 0
```
